Instead, I overcomplicated things a little. However, having three elements allowed for sharper edges (e.g., the chin), and I wanted to try this approach as I always go the "egg-shape route." The result looks nice, but it might not have been worth it.

On the other hand, having different elements permitted some extra customization (like adding transform or inset shadows) that would have been more complicated if it was only one element. So not everything is bad.

Mouth

The mouth is not a simple line. It is also conformed of three parts to give some "texture," so it's not just a flat thing. In this case, I did it by playing with the border widths, but I could have done it with clip-paths (it would have been a pain later with the pseudo-elements) or linear gradients (it would have been a pain later with the shadows). Browsers may display slightly differently, but it is a price I'll pay.

The object is later used to provide some lip shadow with box-shadow: one on top and another on the side.

Ears

Both ears are the same. The trick to quickly flipping an element horizontally is to apply a transform of scaleX(-1) and voilà! It's done!

.ear { /* code to draw the ear: border-radius, backgrounds... */
} /* select the second ear and flip it */
.ear + .ear { transform: scaleX(-1);
}

Once that's done, to make them look slightly different, I rotated the second one at a slightly different angle and also pushed it a little bit farther (from the other side). Combined with the various elements from both sides, that gives the impression of having two different ears.

Eyes

Drawing eyes is always tricky. They make or break the drawing, and the balance between one and the other is subtle. The eyes on this drawing are far from perfect, but they are Ok.

Each eye is two elements: a div (for the eye in itself) and one pseudo-class (for the pupil and iris). The eye will have an overflow:hidden so the pseudo-element doesn't extend beyond its scope.

The pupil and the iris are radial gradients, with more radial gradients for the light reflections and box-shadows to trace small lines on each eye side. However, after duplicating the eye and flipping the second one, there's something different left to do: to avoid having an unwanted symmetry that looks weird (light reflections coming from different directions), the pseudo-element of the second eye needs to be flipped too:

.eye { /* eye styles */
} .eye + .eye { transform: scaleX(-1); /* flip the eye */
} .eye + .eye::before { transform: scaleX(-1); /* flip the iris in the second eye */
}

Neck

The neck element is just going to be a container for its ::before and ::after pseudo-elements, which will be the sides of the neck.

Using box-shadow, the elements' shadow will be the colors of the neck, and it will provide some shadow. Using transform to rotate and skew, the shapes will be softer and look more like a cartoon neck than if I just had a rounded corner.

Hair

The hair consists of eight times the same element with slight variations (in position and size, but sometimes also in the background). I initially used borders and box shadows to draw the bangs, but that was a problem when trying to make them look different (plus, it looks terrible in browsers other than Chrome). Another problem with this approach is that the top part of the hair bangs has an undesired black line on Chrome (it has some trouble at times with things like that.) So I opted for box shadows and gradients to draw the hair:
item.author: alvaromontoro

item.title: 使用 JavaScript 框架的 Freshworks MarketPlace 应用程序 | Freshworks MarketPlace apps using JavaScript Frameworks
item.description:
在这篇文章中，我们将了解如何使用React和Vue等流行的 JavaScript 框架构建Freshworks Marketplace应用程序。

目前只支持这两个流行的框架，我希望在不久的将来 CLI 会支持更多的框架。

当然，在安装 Freshworks CLI 之前，您需要在机器中安装 Node.js。 您可以在此处找到有关安装 CLI 的先决条件的更多信息

npm install https://dl.freshdev.io/cli/fdk.tgz -g

您可以通过运行以下命令来验证 CLI 安装：

您应该获得类似 fdk cli 的已安装版本以及已发布的任何新版本的信息，以便您可以升级。

该库公开了一个名为fdk的可执行文件，您可以使用它来引导 Marketplace 应用程序。 模板

这是通过--template选项提供的，该选项可以传递给 fdk fdk create命令。

如果 create 命令未提供--template选项，系统将提示您选择模板。 首次使用的用户最常用和推荐的模板是your_first_app模板。

如果您正在为 Freshdesk 产品构建应用程序，目前大约有 7-8 个模板可供选择。 每个产品都支持最适合各自产品支持的典型用例的各种模板。 尝试在第一个提示（产品提示）中选择不同的产品，您将看到一组不同的模板。

让我们简要了解一些最广泛使用的模板。

这是一个基本模板，它仅在工单详细信息页面侧边栏中显示您的应用程序的联系信息。 如果您刚开始使用fdk或不知道要使用哪种类型的模板，这是推荐给初学者的模板，以掌握 Marketplace 应用程序的组件。

如果您想为 Marketplace 构建无服务器应用程序，这是您应该使用的入门模板。 使用此模板，您可以构建满足各种用例的应用程序，例如自定义自动化、数据同步、警报和通知。

此模板将向您展示如何构建使用来自Freshworks的 CRM 产品（称为Freshsales ）的客户数据的应用程序。 此示例应用程序将展示可用于与 CRM 产品交互的各种 API 和接口。 如果您不熟悉这些 api，或者您刚刚开始使用 CRM 功能构建应用程序，那么您应该使用此入门模板。

这是另一个示例应用程序模板，它为 Marketplace 应用程序中使用的安装参数提供了一些占位符配置。

安装参数在 Marketplace 用语中也称为iparams ，用于配置应用程序安装。 这些参数通过在将应用程序安装到相应产品中之前动态配置应用程序参数来帮助您微调安装过程。

这是使用React.js作为构建应用程序前端的主要工具来引导应用程序的示例模板。 我将在以下部分向您展示如何使用此模板。

这是使用Vue.js的 2.x 版本作为构建应用程序前端的主要工具来引导应用程序的示例模板。

这是使用最新版本的Vue.js作为构建应用程序前端的主要工具来引导应用程序的示例模板。

反应

要使用 fdk 创建 React 应用程序，您必须使用模板your_first_react_app ，如下所示：

 fdk create --app-dir my-react-mkp-app --products freshdesk --template your_first_react_app

生成的文件夹结构如下所示：

让我们看一下App.js组件内部，了解 React 应用程序内部发生了什么。 首先，应用程序尝试通过组件中 React 的useEffect挂钩将Fresh Client脚本注入到文档的头部。 然后在脚本成功注入后，它通过传递app.initialized承诺返回的client实例来呈现HelloUser组件。 由于每个 Marketplace 应用程序都在 IFrame 内呈现，因此应用程序和父页面之间的所有通信都通过此客户端对象进行。

 import React , { useState , useEffect } from ' react ' ; import ' ./App.css ' ; import HelloUser from ' ./components/HelloUser ' const App = () => { const [ loaded , setLoaded ] = useState ( false ); const [ child , setChild ] = useState ( < h3 > App is loading < /h3> ) useEffect (() => { const script = document . createElement ( ' script ' ); script . src = ' https://static.freshdev.io/fdk/2.0/assets/fresh_client.js ' ; script . addEventListener ( ' load ' , () => setLoaded ( true )); script . defer = true ; document . head . appendChild ( script ); }, []); useEffect (() => { if ( ! loaded ) return app . initialized (). then (( client ) => { setChild (( < HelloUser client = { client } /> ) ) }) }, [ loaded ]) return ( < div > { child } < /div > ) } export default App ;

在HelloUser组件中，我们使用此client实例来获取当前支持代理的详细联系信息，并在组件 UI 中显示代理的名称。

 const HelloUser = ( props ) => { const [ name , setName ] = useState ( '' ) props . client . data . get ( ' contact ' ). then (( data ) => { setName ( data . contact . name ) }) return ( < div > < img src = { icon } className = " App-logo " alt = " logo " /> < img src = { reactLogo } className = " App-logo " alt = " logo " /> < h3 > Hi { name }, < /h3 > < p > Welcome to your first react app in Freshdesk < /p > < /div > ) }

您可以分别使用 Vue.js 的 2.x 和 3.x 版本的模板your_first_vue_app和your_first_vue3_app来使用 Vue.js 构建 Marektplace 应用程序。

对于 Vue 2.x

 fdk create --app-dir my-vue-mkp-app --products freshdesk --template your_first_vue_app

对于 Vue 3

 fdk create --app-dir my-vue3-mkp-app --products freshdesk --template your_first_vue3_app

这就是从示例应用程序模板构建 Vue 应用程序的方式。

而在App组件中，它和我们的 React 应用程序一样，我们通过调用实际使用客户端数据方法的initialize方法来获取 Vue.js 组件的mounted()生命周期钩子中的联系人信息。

 < template > <Sidebar :agentName= "name" /> </ template > < script > import Sidebar from " ./components/Sidebar.vue " ; export default { mounted () { this . initialize (); }, name : " App " , components : { Sidebar , }, data () { return { name : "" , }; }, methods : { initialize () { app . initialized (). then (( client ) => { client . data . get ( " contact " ). then ( ( data ) => { this . name = data . contact . name ; }, ( error ) => { console . error ( " error " , error ); } ); }); }, }, }; </ script > < style scoped > </ style >

示例应用

您可以在 Freshdesk 开发人员文档门户的示例应用程序部分找到使用各种框架构建的各种示例应用程序的 Github 存储库链接。

参考

希望你喜欢这篇文章，并对如何使用fdk cli 使用 React 和 Vue 创建 Marketplace 应用程序有一些基本的了解。 如有任何疑问和帮助，请随时与社区联系。



原文:

In this post, we are going to take a look at how we can build Freshworks Marketplace apps using popular JavaScript frameworks like React and Vue.

The official CLI tool provided by the App Development Platform of Freshworks has built-in scaffolding capabilities for building Marketplace apps using frameworks like React and Vue. At present only these two popular frameworks are being supported, I hope more frameworks will be supported by the CLI in the near future.

Freshworks CLI

The Freshworks CLI is a command-line tool which can be installed using the Node package manager npm. Of course, you need to have Node.js installed in your machine before installing the Freshworks CLI. You can find more information about the pre-requisites for installing the CLI here

npm install https://dl.freshdev.io/cli/fdk.tgz -g

You can verify the CLI installation by running following command:

You should get something like the installed version of your fdk cli and also about any new versions that have been released so that you can upgrade.

The library exposes an executable called fdk using which you can bootstrap Marketplace apps. fdk also comes with pre-built templates for various frameworks and libraries for building the apps without having to setup the project boilerplates from scratch.

Templates

The fdk can take inputs on which type of boilerplate the Marketplace app is going to use. This is provided through the --template option which can be passed to the fdk create command.

If the --template option is not provided with the create command, you are prompted to select a template. The most common and recommended template for first-time users will be the your_first_app template.

At present there are about 7-8 templates available to choose from, if you are building the app for the Freshdesk product. Each and every product supports various kinds of templates best suited for the typical use cases supported by the respective products. Try choosing a different product in the first prompt (the product prompt) and you will be presented with a different set of templates.

Lets have a brief information about what some of the most widely used templates.

your_first_app

This is the basic template which just displays the contact information for your app in the Ticket Details page sidebar. If you are just starting with the fdk or don't know what type of template to use, this is the recommended template for beginners, to get a grip on the components of a Marketplace app.

your_first_serverless_app

If you want to build Serverless apps for the Marketplace, this is the starter template you should be using. With this template, you can build apps which cater to various use cases like custom automations, data synchronization, alerts and notifications.

sample_crm_app

This template will show you how to build apps that uses the Customer data from the CRM product of Freshworks called Freshsales. This sample app will showcase the various apis and interfaces you can use to interface with the CRM product. If you are not familiar with these apis or if your are just starting to build an app using the CRM capabilities, this is the starter template you should be using.

advanced_iparams_app

This is another sample app template which is having some placeholder configuration for the Installation Parameters used in a Marketplace app.

Installation Parameters also known as iparams in the Marketplace parlance are used to configure the app installation. These parameters help you to fine-tune the installation process by dynamically configuring the application parameters before installing the app inside the respective products.

your_first_react_app

This is the sample template for bootstrapping the apps using React.js as the primary tool for building the front-end of the app. I will show you how to use this template in the below sections.

your_first_vue_app

This is the sample template for bootstrapping the apps using the 2.x versions of Vue.js as the primary tool for building the front-end of the app.

your_first_vue3_app

This is the sample template for bootstrapping the apps using the latest version of Vue.js as the primary tool for building the front-end of the app.

React

For creating React apps with fdk you have to use the template your_first_react_app like below:

fdk create --app-dir my-react-mkp-app --products freshdesk --template your_first_react_app

This is how the generated folder structure will look like:

Let's take a look inside the App.js component for what is happening inside the React app. First the app is trying to inject the Fresh Client script into the head of the document through the useEffect hook of React in the component. Then after the script is successfully injected then it is rendering the HelloUser component by passing the client instance returned by the app.initialized promise. Since every Marketplace app is rendered inside an IFrame, all the communication between the app and parent page occurs through this client object.

import React, { useState, useEffect } from 'react';
import './App.css';
import HelloUser from './components/HelloUser' const App = () => { const [loaded, setLoaded] = useState(false); const [child, setChild] = useState(<h3>App is loading</h3>)
 useEffect(() => { const script = document.createElement('script'); script.src = 'https://static.freshdev.io/fdk/2.0/assets/fresh_client.js'; script.addEventListener('load', () => setLoaded(true)); script.defer = true; document.head.appendChild(script); }, []); useEffect(() => { if (!loaded) return app.initialized().then((client) => { setChild((<HelloUser client={client} />))
 }) }, [loaded]) return ( <div> {child} </div>
 )
} export default App;

And inside the HelloUser component, we use this client instance to fetch the contact details of the current support agent and display the agent's name in the component UI.

const HelloUser = (props) => { const [name, setName] = useState('') props.client.data.get('contact').then((data) => { setName(data.contact.name) }) return ( <div> <img src={icon} className="App-logo" alt="logo" /> <img src={reactLogo} className="App-logo" alt="logo" /> <h3>Hi {name},</h3>
 <p>Welcome to your first react app in Freshdesk</p>
 </div>
 )
}

Vue

You can build Marektplace apps using Vue.js by using the templates your_first_vue_app and your_first_vue3_app for the 2.x and 3.x versions of Vue.js respectively.

For Vue 2.x

fdk create --app-dir my-vue-mkp-app --products freshdesk --template your_first_vue_app

For Vue 3

fdk create --app-dir my-vue3-mkp-app --products freshdesk --template your_first_vue3_app

This is how the Vue app is structured from the sample app template.

And in the App component, it is the same like our React app, we fetching the contact information inside the mounted() lifecycle hook of Vue.js components by calling the initialize method which is actually using the client data methods.

<template > <Sidebar :agentName="name" />
</template> <script>
import Sidebar from "./components/Sidebar.vue"; export default { mounted() { this.initialize(); }, name: "App", components: { Sidebar, }, data() { return { name: "", }; }, methods: { initialize() { app.initialized().then((client) => { client.data.get("contact").then( (data) => { this.name = data.contact.name; }, (error) => { console.error("error", error); } ); }); }, },
};
</script> <style scoped>
</style>

Sample apps

You can find the Github repository links of various sample apps built with various frameworks in the Sample Apps section of the Freshdesk Developers documentation portal.

References

Hope you enjoyed the post, and had some basic understanding on how to use the fdk cli to create Marketplace apps using React and Vue. Please feel free to reach out the community for any queries and help.
item.author: rajasegar

item.title: 为开发人员介绍新的入门指南 Azure Skills Navigator | Introducing the new ramp-up guide for developers, Azure Skills Navigator
item.description:
从 0 到 Cloud 的 4 个技术栈（C#、Java、Python 和 JavaScript）的技能指南。

我们很高兴推出新的 Azure Skills Navigator，这是我们自己的指南，可帮助开发人员开始使用 Azure。

我们拥有大量资源来帮助刚接触云的开发人员，本指南将把我们所有一流的内容集中在一个地方，让查找和导航变得更容易。

资源

为什么本指南

在当今竞争激烈的市场中保持领先并继续学习新技能非常重要，尤其是在最近对云技术专业技能的需求激增的情况下。

我们听到你的声音，我们在这里为您提供帮助。 我们的 Azure Skills Navigator 指南可确保您在开始探索 Azure 时打下坚实的基础。

指南中有什么？

本指南利用各种 Microsoft 资源绘制了加深您的知识的路线，分为三个阶段：

如何阅读指南

该指南包含三个阶段，因此可以使用编程语言、应用程序框架或云开始您的学习之旅。

  • 加速- 发现核心技能和语言基础。
  • 应用程序开发——了解如何使用各种云架构、Web 框架、移动应用程序和相关 Azure 服务构建云原生应用程序。
  • 在生产中——探索用于监控、日志记录、检测和授权的服务。

选择您的技术冒险，.NET、Java、Node.js 或 Python

我们的指南概述了 Java、.NET、Node.js 和 Python 的关键概念，这些是在 Microsoft Azure 上建立坚实基础的关键主题。

多模式、文本和视频

学习之旅由多种形式组成——虚拟培训、学习课程、自定进度模块、视频、文档、认证等——让您可以在自己的时间学习。 在您打下坚实的基础后，我们将帮助您浏览我们的中级和高级内容，以便继续学习。

下载指南并立即开始学习

学到更多

要了解有关这些指南的更多信息，请查看 Microsoft Build 上的 OnDemand Intro to Tech 会议：由 Christoffer Noring、Nitya Narasimhan 和 Someleze Diko 主持的云的新开发人员指南。



原文:

A skill guide in 4 tech stacks, C#, Java, Python and JavaScript, from 0 to Cloud.

We are excited to introduce the new Azure Skills Navigator, our very own guide to help developers get started on Azure.

We have a large collection of resources to help developers new to the cloud, and this guide will gather all our best-in-class content in one place, making it easier to find and navigate.

Resources

Why this guide

It is very important to stay ahead of the tech curve and continue to learn new skills in today’s competitive market, especially with the recent surge in demand for professionals skills in cloud technologies.

We hear you, and we are here to help. Our Azure Skills Navigator guide ensures that you have a solid foundation as you begin to explore Azure.

What's in the guide?

Azure Skills Navigator will provide a unique learning journey designed for developers building apps with low-code techniques to simplify, automate, and transform business tasks and processes. This guide maps out your itinerary for deepening your knowledge, drawing on a wide array of Microsoft resources, divided into three phases:

How to read the guide

The guide contains three phases, so start your learning journey where you are, either with a programming language, an app framework or the cloud.

  • Ramp up – Discover core skills and language fundamentals.
  • App development – Learn how to build cloud-native apps with various cloud architectures, frameworks for web, mobile apps, and relevant Azure services.
  • In production – Explore services for monitoring, logging, instrumentation, and authorization.

Choose your tech adventure, .NET, Java, Node.js or Python

Our guide provides an overview of key concepts across Java, .NET, Node.js, and Python, crucial topics to establishing a strong foundation on Microsoft Azure.

Multi modal, text and videos

The learning journey is comprised of a variety of formats – virtual trainings, learning courses, self-paced modules, videos, documentations, certifications, and more – that allow you to learn at your own time. After you have built a solid foundation, we will help you navigate our intermediate and advanced level content for continued learning.

Download the guide and start learning today

Learn more

To learn more about these guides check out the OnDemand Intro to Tech session at Microsoft Build: The New Developer’s Guide to the Cloud hosted by Christoffer Noring, Nitya Narasimhan, and Someleze Diko.
item.author: softchris

item.title: 面向 SaaS 创始人的敏捷软件开发生命周期阶段 | Agile Software Development Life Cycle Phases for SaaS Founders
item.description:
原文由 SoftFormance 撰写

您最近可能经常听到“敏捷”这个词。 这种做法在 IT 领域迅速变得越来越流行，主要是在谈论软件开发时。

事实上，根据第 15 届年度敏捷状态报告，在过去的 15 年中，采用敏捷实践和流程的公司数量稳步增加。

这种流行有一个明确的原因，即敏捷允许企业所有者和开发人员找到最灵活、最有效的方式来在最短的时间内交付软件。

企业主选择敏捷是为了节省时间和金钱，并优先考虑组织内所有团队之间的有效沟通和合作。

当你开始着手一个新项目时，时间是你最宝贵的资源，你绝对不想浪费它。 您可能不希望您的测试人员团队坐等开发人员完成软件工作。

这里的关键是分配您手头的资源，以便团队的每个部分同时参与该过程。

这就是为什么在 SoftFormance，我们在与所有客户合作时一直在实践软件开发生命周期敏捷的原因。 这种做法使我们能够提高公司内部和客户的工作效率。

如果您想按时、按预算且没有压力地完成您的软件开发项目，请继续阅读。

什么是敏捷？
敏捷就是对变化的适应性。 随着市场趋势的迅速变化，企业主必须能够立即对其做出反应，以保持竞争力并吸引新客户。

这就是敏捷派上用场的时候——采用这种做法使我们即使在最具挑战性的环境中也能取得成功，并找到一种方法来保护公司的资源。

关键的敏捷实践包括自适应软件开发、精益软件开发、极限编程、Crystal、看板、Scrum 等。


方法的选择取决于开发机构和客户的要求。 尽管如此，它们都是灵活的、可迭代的，并且在最短的时间内提供出色的结果方面非常有效。

敏捷在其他方法中脱颖而出，因为它专注于工作人员以及他们如何合作。 这一切都与团队合作、自组织和协作有关。

根据敏捷报告，IT 团队采用敏捷实践提高了客户满意度，对业务价值产生了积极影响，并帮助实现了业务目标。

敏捷方法的类型
有几种类型的敏捷方法可以适应各种项目并帮助每个企业主实现他们的目标。 尽管如此，一切都基于敏捷宣言的原则。

根据它，无论您选择哪种方法，您都应该始终优先考虑个人和互动，与客户合作，创建工作软件，并对变化做出响应。

在 2021 年的报告中，Scrum 被列为最常用的敏捷方法，有 66% 的人使用它。 其他敏捷方法包括自适应软件开发、精益软件开发、极限编程、水晶、看板等。

让我们来谈谈他们中的每一个。

正如 2020 年 Scrum 指南中所述，Scrum 是一个框架，可帮助人员、团队和组织通过针对复杂问题的自适应解决方案创造价值。


团队在 Scrum 中非常重要。 它通常由一名 Scrum Master、一名产品负责人和开发人员组成。 在这个团队中，没有层次结构。 每个人对最终结果的影响都是平等的，每个人都专注于产品目标。

敏捷方法步骤分为通常持续两周的冲刺。 每个 sprint 都是一个迭代，它逐渐将原始草稿项目带入完成的项目。

此外，他们能够自我组织和自我管理，这意味着他们可以决定做什么、何时以及如何做。

每个团队成员的职责是什么？

开发人员在每个 sprint 期间创建可用的工作软件。

产品负责人负责制定和传达产品目标，传达产品待办事项，订购它们，并确保流程透明并为每个人所理解。

此人还对团队的效率负责。

看板
根据 Kanbanize 的说法，看板是一种可以帮助您将工作可视化、最大限度地提高效率并不断改进的方法。 所有工作都在看板板上表示。 它使您可以跨团队优化工作流程并跟踪进度。


最初，看板是作为制造系统引入的，但后来转移到了软件开发团队。

由于所有工作流程都呈现在看板板上并与所有团队共享，因此看板需要彻底的互动和透明度才能带来最佳结果。

极限编程 (XP)
敏捷方法的下一个阶段侧重于团队合作、反馈、沟通和客户满意度。 就像 Scrum 一样，XP 也使用 sprint。

正如 Don Wells 所描述的，当 XP 适合时有以下特征：

动态变化的软件需求 由于时间紧迫或系统对团队来说是一个新的挑战而导致的项目风险 程序员小组 能够创建自动化的单元和功能测试

极限编程通常涉及 5 个敏捷方法阶段：规划、设计、编码、测试和倾听。


与 XP 相关的角色如下：

客户 程序员 追踪者或经理 教练

这种敏捷方法不是通用的，但如果它适合您的项目，XP 将帮助您创建稳定、性能良好的系统、清晰简洁的代码、节省时间、减少文档，并确保高客户参与度和满意度。

自适应软件开发
除了三大方法论（Scrum、极限编程和 Kaban），一些程序员还使用 ASD（自适应软件开发）。


它是最早的敏捷方法之一。 根据 ASD 的创建者 Jim Highsmith 和 Sam Bayer 的说法，在复杂的软件项目中取得成功的唯一方法是在整个项目中不断学习，而不是固守僵化的计划。

在这次敏捷实践中，敏捷方法论分为三个步骤：推测、协作和学习。


第一阶段，投机，也称为计划阶段。 下一阶段是关于协作：开发人员和软件的最终用户之间以及团队内部。 最后，在学习阶段，参与该过程的每个人都在朝着最终结果努力的过程中不断学习。

精益软件开发
精益软件开发 (LSD) 是开发人员常用的另一种敏捷实践。 它基于优化开发时间和资源、消除浪费并准确交付产品所需的内容。


它也被称为最小可行产品 (MVP) 策略。 它是一种功能数量有限的产品，至少可以解决目标客户的一个问题。

首先，其简化的方法允许开发人员在更短的时间内交付功能更强大的产品。

其次，它允许程序员团队做出决定，并积极影响士气和生产力。

最后，LSD 消除了不必要的活动并有助于节省成本。

尽管如此，它并不适合每个项目，因为精益软件开发依赖于所涉及的团队。 因此，它不像 Scrum 那样具有可扩展性，例如。

还有很多关于 LSD 的文档，许多企业主宁愿避免使用它，以防止开发错误。

水晶
最后，我们要讨论的最后一个敏捷方法是 Crystal。 该框架侧重于个人及其互动，而不是工具和流程。

这种方法有两个主要原则：

团队可以自行决定如何优化他们的工作 由于每个项目都在不断变化并且需要独特的方法，因此只有团队才能决定如何处理它。

这种方法的主要优势在于它允许团队找到最有效的工作方式，促进透明度和开放式沟通，并允许他们快速适应变化。

但是，缺乏文档可能会让一些企业主和开发人员感到困惑，并且 Crystal 不适合需要彻底规划的项目。

现在我们已经讨论了主要的敏捷方法，让我们简要讨论一下敏捷宣言背后的主要原则，然后进入敏捷软件开发阶段。

敏捷宣言背后的原则
敏捷有 12 条主要原则，它们在敏捷宣言中有所描述。 让我们在这里总结一下。

通过早期和持续的软件交付使客户满意。 变化总是受欢迎的。 在最短的时间内交付工作软件。 开发人员必须每天与业务人员协作。 项目应该围绕有动力的个人来构建。 面对面的交谈是有效沟通的关键。 工作软件是衡量进度的标准。 敏捷促进可持续发展。 注重技术卓越和有效设计。 越简单越好。 自组织团队是最有效的。 团队定期反思他们的进步，以调整他们的行为并与其他团队保持一致。

任何想要创建他们的软件并使用敏捷方法的企业主都应该虔诚地遵循这 12 个步骤。

他们指导团队的工作，帮助他们专注于重要的事情，甚至管理最复杂的项目。

敏捷软件开发生命周期 (SDLC)
在我谈论 sprint 周期和四个敏捷仪式之前，让我们回顾一下关键的软件开发生命周期阶段。 我们已经在新产品开发文章中详细讨论了它们，所以让我们在这里总结一下敏捷项目的关键阶段。

创意产生

这是敏捷产品生命周期的第一阶段。 您的目标是回答三个主要问题：

问题是什么？ 谁有这个问题？

为什么这是个问题？


在敏捷周期的这一步中，您需要找到可以用您的软件解决的问题，并在两种主要方法中进行选择：PIDD 和 Software Safari。

如果您想了解更多关于这些方法以及如何产生有效想法的信息，请阅读这篇文章。

敏捷阶段的下一个阶段是构建您的产品策略。

在开发软件之前，您需要制定详细的产品策略，其中包括有关用户获取、用户激活、用户保留、用户推荐和收入模式的数据。

在敏捷项目生命周期中构建应用程序和创建新软件时，您需要知道如何获得新用户，让他们留下并经常使用您的产品，并确保您的收入模式有效。

敏捷 SDLC 阶段的下一个阶段是项目路线图。

如果您想按时、在预算范围内、以适当的质量和范围推出产品，您需要一个项目路线图。

它包括三个主要步骤：技术监督、规范和估算。

在技​​术监督步骤中，您确定项目在技术上是否可行。


然后，您创建一个规范列表并向开发人员解释应该做什么。


最后，您估计项目的时间表、预算和里程碑。

敏捷生命周期的下一步是 UI/UX 设计。 简而言之，产品设计是您的软件从用户的角度来看的外观和工作方式。

这是一个多步骤的过程，因此，如果您想了解更多信息，我们为您提供详细指南。

当您创建软件产品时，您不会为第一个版本构建功能齐全的软件。 相反，您创建了一个可以出售的更简单的版本。 之后，您将继续添加越来越多的功能。
第一个小版本称为最小可销售产品，或 MSP。 如果您想了解更多信息，请阅读我们为您准备的材料。

在敏捷生命周期的这个阶段，您启动了新的 MSP。 有几个关键步骤需要遵循，主要是：

拳头用户

  1. 发布后软件物流

即使您的软件已经开发完成，也不是最后阶段。 发布后，您需要专注于修复错误并为您的产品添加新功能。

在发布后的软件物流阶段，您应该花时间支持和维护您的产品以及吸引新客户。

如果您想了解有关这七个步骤的更多信息，请阅读此详细指南。

在 SoftFormance，设计和开发阶段都遵循明确的敏捷方法论，主要是四个敏捷仪式，我们将在下面讨论。

敏捷仪式（四个阶段）
敏捷，主要是 Scrum，有四个主要的仪式，将其变为现实，并帮助最大限度地利用开发过程。

这些仪式被称为：

冲刺计划每日站立冲刺回顾

冲刺回顾


这些仪式是不同的，但它们的目标相同——将团队聚集在一起，帮助他们更有效地协作，并快速完成工作。

为什么我们需要这四个仪式？

好吧，它们可以帮助组织更加灵活和适应变化。 因此，这些仪式可以帮助您成功。

它们允许您分小部分计划工作，如果您需要经常改变方向、调整和纠正一些错误，这非常有用。

让我们详细谈谈每个仪式。

冲刺计划
这是第一个敏捷 SDLC 方法，它确保团队的每个成员都了解 sprint 目标。


接下来是什么？

在 sprint 计划结束时，开发团队必须清楚地了解他们在 sprint 结束时能够完成什么。 这也称为冲刺目标。

如果您想成功完成 Spring Planning 步骤，请确保专注于协作，了解团队的步伐，关注产品待办事项，并将用户故事分成小任务分发给开发团队。

每日站立
每日站立的主要目标是团结团队并将每个人聚集在一起。 这段时间用来分享当天的计划，讨论可能出现的问题。


这些会议通常持续 15 分钟，并且每天在同一时间举行。

在日常站立期间，Scrum Master 应该解决所有减慢工作流程的问题和问题，甚至在需要时更改开发过程。

冲刺回顾
在冲刺之后，是时候审查工作并收集团队外部人员的反馈了。 此过程有助于与利益相关者建立信任并改进未来的开发过程。


在此阶段不要展示任何未完成的工作，这一点很重要。 此外，您不仅应该关注产品功能，还应该关注客户价值、用户体验和商业价值。

冲刺回顾
最后的仪式是冲刺回顾。 在此阶段，您分析已经完成的工作，并尝试确定下一次需要改进的地方。 这一步应该帮助团队清楚地了解犯了哪些错误以及将来如何避免这些错误。 在 sprint 回顾步骤中，团队为这些问题生成解决方案并制定行动计划。

为什么选择远程敏捷团队？
大多数敏捷软件开发周期过程都是由远程或外包的专门团队执行的。 出于各种原因，企业主更愿意与第三方机构合作，而不是雇用内部开发团队。

例如，将您的软件开发外包可以帮助您更明智地管理财务、节省时间并在来自世界各地的数千名才华横溢的专家中进行选择。

我们已经在本文中介绍了外包的优缺点，但让我们回顾一下为什么要选择远程敏捷团队。

控制成本

外包使您能够控制费用并决定在哪个领域进行更多投资。

进入全球人才库
外包允许公司从世界各地聘请敏捷开发团队，并在众多有才华的专家中进行选择。

缩短交货时间
一个大型的敏捷外包团队可以让您比内部团队更快地完成项目。

提高效率
外包允许您将特定任务委派给专门从事您所需服务的专家。 结果，它提高了生产力，因为每个员工都专注于他们最擅长的事情。

专注于核心业务活动
外包的另一个好处是它可以让你专注于你擅长的领域并委派其余的任务。

增加人员配备的灵活性
聘请外部开发团队可以帮助您随时轻松地减少或扩展您的劳动力，从优秀人才中进行选择，并确保每个项目都委派给专业人士。

获得创新
通过与有才华的外部开发人员一起扩大您的员工队伍，您会欢迎尚未提出的针对您的业务的新想法和解决方案。

更好的风险管理
外包还可以降低长期雇用某人时做出错误决定的风险。 通过外包，您可以在新人才中进行选择，测试他们的技能和专业知识，并选择最适合您业务目标的人才。

如果您想了解更多关于每个好处、外包风险以及如何减轻它们的信息，请阅读这篇博文。

包起来
在本指南中，您了解了敏捷开发周期以及如何应用敏捷方法在最短的时间内开发工作软件。 这种做法可以让你的团队高效合作，适应变化，应对意外的变化和场景。

无论您选择哪种敏捷实践（Scrum、看板、极限编程或 Crystal），您都会立即看到开发过程的积极变化，因为它将更加灵活，文档更少，规则更严格，协作性更强。

如果您有创建软件产品或移动应用程序的想法，并且您的目标是获得快速且可预测的结果 - 我们随时准备为您提供帮助。

我们的 SoftFormace 团队多年来一直使用敏捷理念来构建我们客户的软件，结果表明这是迄今为止快速取得最大成果的最有效方法。

但是，如果您还没有准备好开始您的软件创意并且有一些问题或意见 - 请随时在下面发表评论，我们会尽快回复您。

菊池拓哉—— 托尼·雷日科夫 - Aug 27 '21 21 年 8 月 27 日



原文:

The original article was written by SoftFormance

You have probably heard the term “Agile” a lot recently. This practice is quickly getting more and more popular in the IT sector, mainly when talking about software development.

In fact, according to the 15th Annual State Of Agile Report, during the last 15 years, there has been a steady increase in the number of companies adopting Agile practices and processes.

There’s a clear reason for this popularity, and it’s that Agile allows business owners and developers to find the most flexible and effective way to deliver software in the shortest terms.

Business owners choose Agile to save time and money and prioritize effective communication and cooperation within all teams within the organization.

When you start working on a new project, time is your most precious resource, and there’s no way you would want to waste it. You probably don’t want your team of testers to sit around waiting for developers to finish working on software.

The key here is to distribute the resources you have at hand so that every part of the team is engaged in the process at the same time.

That is the reason why at SoftFormance, we have been practicing the software development life cycle Agile when working with all our clients. This practice allows us to increase the efficiency of our work both within our company and with our clients.

If you want to finish your software development project on time, on budget, and without stress, read on.

What is Agile?
Agile is all about adaptiveness to change. As the trends in the market are changing quickly, business owners must be able to react to them instantly to stay competitive and attract new clients.

That is when Agile comes at hand – adopting this practice allows us to succeed even in the most challenging environment and find a way to preserve the company’s resources.

The key Agile practices include Adaptive Software Development, Lean Software Development, Extreme Programming, Crystal, Kanban, Scrum, and others.


The choice of the methodology depends on the development agency and the requirements of the client. Still, all of them are flexible, iterative, and incredibly effective in providing great results in the shortest terms.

Agile stands out among other approaches because it focuses on people doing the work and how they cooperate. It’s all about teamwork, self-organization, and collaboration.

According to the Agile Report, the adoption of Agile practices in IT teams has increased customer satisfaction, positively impacted business values, and helped achieve business objectives.

Types of Agile Methodologies
There are several types of Agile methodology to fit every kind of project and help every business owner achieve their goals. Still, everything is based on the principles of the Agile manifesto.

According to it, no matter what methodology you choose, you should always prioritize individuals and interactions, collaborate with customers, create working software, and be responsive to change.

In the 2021 report, Scrum ranked as the most used Agile approach, with 66% of people using it. Other Agile approaches include Adaptive Software Development, Lean Software Development, Extreme Programming, Crystal, Kanban, and others.

Let’s talk about each of them.

Scrum
As mentioned in the 2020 Scrum Guide, Scrum is a framework that helps people, teams, and organizations generate value through adaptive solutions for complex problems.


A team is very important in Scrum. It usually consists of one Scrum Master, one Product Owner, and the Developer. Within this team, there are no hierarchies. Everyone has an equal impact on the final result, and everyone is focused on the Product Goal.

The Agile methodology steps are divided into sprints that usually last up to two weeks. Each sprint is an iteration that brings a raw draft project to a finished one gradually.

Each member of the Scrum Team has all skills necessary to participate in every sprint. Moreover, they are able to self-organize and self-manage, which means that they decide what to do, when, and how.

What are the responsibilities of each team member?

Developers create usable working software during each sprint.

The Product Owner is responsible for developing and communicating the Product Goal, communicating Product Backlog items, ordering them, and ensuring that the process is transparent and understood by everyone.

The Scrum Master helps everyone understand what Scrum is, its principles, and its philosophy. This person is also accountable for the team’s effectiveness.

Kanban
Another popular Agile methodology in SDLC is Kanban. According to Kanbanize, Kanban is a method that helps you visualize work, maximize efficiency, and improve continuously. All work is represented on kanban boards. It allows you to optimize your work process across the teams and track the progress.


Initially, Kanban was introduced as a manufacturing system, but it moved to software development teams.

Since all work processes are presented on the Kanban Board and shared with all teams, Kanban requires thorough interaction and transparency to bring the best results.

Extreme Programming (XP)
The next of the phases of agile methodology focuses on teamwork, feedback, communication, and customer satisfaction. Just like Scrum, XP also uses sprints.

There are the characteristics when XP is appropriate, as described by Don Wells:

Dynamically changing software requirements Project risks caused by tight schedules or if the system is a new challenge for a team Small groups of programmers Ability to create automated unit and functional tests

Extreme Programming normally involves 5 Agile methodology phases: planning, designing, coding, testing, and listening.


The roles associated with XP are as follows:

Customers Programmers Trackers or managers Coaches

This Agile methodology is not universal, but if it suits your projects, XP will help you create stable, well-performing systems, a clear and concise code, save time, reduce documentation, and ensure high customer engagement and satisfaction.

Adaptive Software Development
Apart from the big three methodologies (Scrum, Extreme Programming, and Kaban), some programmers also use ASD (Adaptive Software Development).


It’s one of the earliest agile methodologies. ASD focuses on collaboration with the clients and encourages developers not to be afraid of uncertainties and interesting traits in future projects.

According to Jim Highsmith and Sam Bayer, the creators of ASD, the only way to achieve success with complex software projects is to continuously learn throughout the project while not sticking to rigid plans.

During this Agile practice, there are three steps for Agile methodology: speculation, collaboration, and learning.


The first phase, speculation, is also known as the planning phase. The next stage is all about collaboration: between developers and end-users of software and within the teams. Finally, during the learning stage, everyone involved in the process continuously learns as they are working towards the end result.

Lean Software Development
Lean Software Development (LSD) is another Agile practice commonly used by developers. It is based on optimizing the development time and resources, eliminating waste, and delivering exactly what the product needs.


It is also known as the Minimum Viable Product (MVP) strategy. It’s a product with a limited number of features that solves at least one issue of your target customer.

What are the main strengths of the LSD methodology?

Firstly, its streamlined approach allows developers to deliver a more functional product in less time.

Secondly, it allows the team of programmers to make decisions, and it positively influences morale and productivity.

Finally, LSD eliminates unnecessary activity and helps save costs.

Still, it’s not suitable for every project because Lean Software Development depends on the team involved. Thus, it’s not as scalable as Scrum, for example.

There’s also a lot of documentation on LSD, and many business owners would rather avoid it to prevent development mistakes.

Crystal
Finally, the last Agile methodology we’ll talk about is Crystal. This framework focuses on individuals and their interactions rather than on tools and processes.

This methodology has two main principles:

Teams can determine how to optimize their work on their own Since every project is constantly changing and requires a unique approach, only the team can determine how to deal with it.

The main strengths of this methodology are that it allows the teams to find the way of work that will be the most effective, it promotes transparency and open communication, and it allows them to adapt to changes quickly.

However, the lack of documentation can be confusing for some business owners and developers, and Crystal is not suitable for projects that require thorough planning.

Now that we have talked about the main Agile methodologies let’s briefly discuss the main principles behind the Agile Manifesto and then move on to the Agile software development phases.

Principles behind the Agile Manifesto
There are 12 main principles of Agile, and they are described in the Agile Manifesto. Let’s summarize them here.

Customer satisfaction through early and continuous delivery of software. Changes are always welcomed. Delivering working software in the shortest timescale. Developers must collaborate with business people daily. Projects should be built around motivated individuals. A face-to-face conversation is key to effective communication. Working software is the measure of progress. Agile promotes sustainable development. Attention to technical excellence and effective design. The simpler, the better. Self-organizing teams are the most effective. Teams reflect on their progress regularly to adjust their behavior and tune in with other teams.

Any business owner who wants to create their software and use Agile methodology should follow these 12 steps religiously.

They guide the work of the teams, help them focus on the important things, and manage even the most complicated projects.

Agile Software Development Life-Cycle (SDLC)
Before I talk about sprint cycles and the four Agile ceremonies, let’s recollect the key software development life-cycle stages. We have already talked about them in detail in the New Product Development article, so let’s summarize the key Agile project phases here.

Idea generation

This is the very first stage of the Agile product life cycle. Your goal here is to answer the three main questions:

What is the problem? Who has this problem?

Why is this a problem?


During this step of Agile cycle, you need to find a problem that can be solved with your software and choose among two main approaches: PIDD and Software Safari.

If you want to learn more about these approaches and how to generate valid ideas, read this piece.

The next of the Agile phases is building your product strategy.

Before developing your software, you need to have a detailed product strategy that includes data about user acquisition, user activation, user retention, user referral, and revenue model.

When building an app and creating new software during the Agile project life cycle, you need to know how you will acquire new users, make them stay and use your product frequently, and ensure that your revenue model is effective.

The next of the Agile SDLC phases is project roadmapping.

If you want to launch your product on time, within budget, and of proper quality and scope, you need a project roadmap.

It consists of the three main steps: Technical Oversight, Specification, and Estimates.

During the Technical Oversight step, you determine whether the project is technically doable.


Then, you create a list of specifications and explain what should be done to the developers.


Finally, you estimate the timeline, budget, and milestones of your project.

The next step of Agile lifecycle is UI/UX design. In short, product design is how your software looks and works from the user’s perspective.

It is a multi-step process, so if you want to learn more about it, we have a detailed guide for you.

When you’re creating a software product, you don’t build full-featured software for the first release. Instead, you create a simpler version of it that can be sold. After that, you keep on adding more and more features.
This first small release is called a Minimum Sellable Product, or an MSP. If you want to learn more about it, read the material we have prepared for you.

During this stage of Agile life cycle, you launch your new MSP. There are key steps to be followed, mainly:

MSP Launch Tech Checklist

MSP Launch Marketing Checklist
The fist users

  1. Post-Launch Software Logistics

Even when your software has been developed, it’s not the final stage. After the launch, you need to focus on fixing bugs and adding new features to your product.

During the post-launch software logistics stage, you should spend time on support & maintenance of your product and on attracting new clients.

If you want to learn more about these seven steps, read this detailed guide.

At SoftFormance, both the design and development stages follow a clear Agile methodology, mainly the four Agile ceremonies, which we will talk about next.

Agile Ceremonies (The Four Stages)
Agile, mainly Scrum, has four main ceremonies that bring it to life and help achieve the most out of the development process.

These ceremonies are known as:

Sprint Planning Daily Stand-Up Sprint Review

Sprint Retrospective


These ceremonies are different, but they have the same goal – to bring teams together, help them collaborate more effectively, and get things done quickly.

Why do we need these four ceremonies?

Well, they help organizations be more flexible and adaptable to change. As a result, these ceremonies can help you succeed.

They allow you to plan work in small portions, which is great if you need to change directions frequently, adjust, and correct some mistakes.

Let’s talk about each ceremony in detail.

Sprint Planning
This is the first Agile SDLC methodology, and it ensures that every member of the team understands the sprint goals.


What comes next?

By the end of the sprint planning, the development team must have a clear understanding of what they will be able to complete by the end of the sprint. This is also known as the sprint goal.

If you want to complete the Spring Planning step successfully, make sure to focus on collaboration, be aware of your team’s pace, focus on the product backlog, and divide user stories into small tasks to distribute among the development team.

Daily Stand-Up
The main goal of daily stand-ups is to unite the team and bring everyone together. This time is used to share the plans for the day and talk about issues that may have arisen.


These meetings usually last up to 15 minutes and are held at the same time every day.

During daily stand-ups, the Scrum Master should address all the questions and issues that slow down the workflow s and even change the development process if needed.

Sprint Review
After the sprints, it is time to review the work and gather feedback from people outside of the team. This process helps build trust with the stakeholders and improve the development process in the future.


It is important not to showcase any incomplete work during this stage. Moreover, you should focus not only on product functionality but also on customer value, user experience, and business value.

Sprint Retrospective
The final ceremony is a sprint retrospective. During this stage, you analyze what has been done and try to identify what needs to be improved the next time. Sprint retrospective helps mitigate possible risks and errors.


This step should help the team clearly understand what mistakes have been made and how to avoid them in the future. During the sprint retrospective step, the team generates solutions to these problems and creates an action plan.

Why choose a remote Agile team?
Most Agile software development cycle processes are performed by remote, or outsourced, dedicated teams. Business owners prefer to work with third-party agencies rather than employing in-house development teams for various reasons.

For example, outsourcing your software development helps you manage your finances more wisely, save time, and choose among thousands of talented experts from all over the world.

We have already covered the advantages and disadvantages of outsourcing in this article, but let’s recollect why you should choose a remote Agile team.

Controlled costs

Outsourcing gives you the ability to control the expenses and decide in which area to invest more.

Access to the global talent pool
Outsourcing allows companies to hire Agile development teams from all over the world and choose among numbers of talented experts.

Reduced delivery time
A large Agile outsourcing team would allow you to finish the project much faster than it would take for the in-house team.

Increased efficiency
Outsourcing allows you to delegate specific tasks to experts who specialize in the services you require. As a result, it boosts productivity because each employee is focused on what they do best.

Focus on core business activities
Another benefit of outsourcing is that it allows you to focus on the area you are good at and delegate the rest of the tasks.

Increased staffing flexibility
Hiring an external development team helps you reduce or extend your workforce easily at any time, choose among great talent, and ensure that each project is delegated to a professional.

Access to innovation
By expanding your workforce with talented external developers, you welcome new ideas and solutions for your business that haven’t been proposed yet.

Better risk management
Outsourcing also reduces the risks of making the wrong decision when hiring someone long-term. With outsourcing, you can choose among new talents, test their skills and expertise, and choose the best one for your business goals.

If you want to learn more about each of the benefits, outsourcing risks, and how to mitigate them, read this blog post.

Wrapping Up
In this guide, you have learned about the Agile development cycle and how to apply Agile methodologies to develop working software in the shortest period of time. This practice allows your team to cooperate efficiently, be adaptable to change, and cope with unexpected changes and scenarios.

No matter what Agile practice you choose – Scrum, Kanban, Extreme Programming, or Crystal – you will instantly see the positive changes to the development process because it will be more flexible, with less documentation and strict rules, and more collaborative.

If you have an idea to create your software product or a mobile app, and your goal is to have a quick and predictable result – we are ready to help you.

Our SoftFormace team has been using Agile philosophy for years to build our clients’ software, and the results show that it is by far the most effective way to achieve the greatest results quickly.

However, if you’re not yet ready to start working on your software idea and have some questions or remarks – feel free to comment down below, and we will respond to you shortly.

Takuya Kikuchi - Aug 31 '21

Tony Ryzhikov - Aug 27 '21
item.author: kateryna_pakhomova

item.title: 简而言之，助推器授权 | Booster authorization in a nutshell
item.description:
在使用了 Booster 框架的几个成功项目之后，有各种各样的客户和内部项目，一个常见的反复出现的问题是 Booster 如何处理授权？
在本文中，我将通过剖析 Booster 的授权方法来阐明这个主题。

标准，标准，标准

按照设计，Booster Framework 是一个基于角色的授权框架，这意味着命令和读取操作是由用户创建的特定角色执行的，即管理员、客户、用户等。 如果您想保持 API 开放，您可以使用预定义的all角色，但这不是一个好主意。 更好的做法是限制对 API 的访问。 如果您想了解更多关于如何在 Booster 中定义角色的信息，请单击此处。

传输安全信息和授权用户的一种广泛采用的标准方式是使用JWT 令牌。

在本文中，我不想深入探讨 JWT 标准的细节，但长话短说，您将需要一个 JWT 身份验证提供程序，或者创建自己的身份验证提供程序，为您的用户生成有效令牌。 在这些令牌中包括您在 Booster 应用程序中作为声明所需的用户角色。

在 Booster 开发的早期阶段，由于我们使用 AWS 作为我们的第一个云提供商，Booster 提供了端点来使用AWS Cognito创建、更新和登录用户。 创建用户时，您必须将该用户的角色指定为相关信息的一部分。 当用户登录时，Cognito 返回一个 JWT 令牌，其中包含与用户相关的所有信息，包括角色。

然后，您必须在 API 请求的 Authorization 标头中使用该令牌作为不记名令牌，并且 Booster 在内部再次调用 Cognito 以验证令牌是否有效，以及它是否包含执行 API 调用的正确角色。

这种方法工作了一段时间，直到一些 Booster 用户不想使用 Cognito。 有些人想使用他们的身份验证，而另一些人则使用不同的身份验证提供程序，例如 Auth0、Firebase、Cognito、Okta 或其他。

再一次的标准来救援。 即使我们使用 JWT，Booster 也与 Cognito 紧密耦合，以验证令牌并获取与其相关的信息。 我们决定提取该部分并在 Booster 核心中使用标准令牌验证，无论您使用哪个提供商，它都适用于 JWT 令牌。

旁注：我们从 Booster 中删除了 Cognito 依赖项，但如果您仍想使用它，您可以包含AWS Auth Rocket ，它为 Booster 应用程序提供开箱即用的最常见 Cognito 功能。

令牌验证者

所以基本上，公钥是客户端众所周知的，但私钥必须是秘密的，并且只能在服务器端使用。

好的...但是我需要在 Booster 中使用什么身份验证？

在 Booster 中，您需要指定令牌验证器。 您可以根据用例使用多个，但目前，让我们专注于使用一个：

 Booster . configure ( ' production ' , ( config : BoosterConfig ): void => { config . tokenVerifiers = [ { jwksUri : ' https://demoapp.firebase.com/.well-known/jwks.json ' , issuer : ' https://securetoken.google.com/demoapp ' , rolesClaim : ' firebase:groups ' , } ] })

在上面的示例中，我们使用 Firebase 作为演示应用的提供者。 让我们使用 Cognito 提供程序创建一个配置：

 Booster . configure ( ' production ' , ( config : BoosterConfig ): void => { config . tokenVerifiers = [ { jwksUri : ' https://cognito-idp.{region}.amazonaws.com/{userPoolId}/.well-known/jwks.json ' , issuer : ' https://cognito-idp.{region}.amazonaws.com/{userPoolId} ' , rolesClaim : ' cognito:groups ' , } ] })

这里也一样，没什么奇怪的，除了提供者特定的配置。

这很好，但是如果我想在开发过程中测试我的角色并且我不想在没有任何身份验证提供程序的情况下坚持下去。

在 Booster 中，您可以使用出色的本地提供程序进行测试，并将其与本地令牌验证器一起配置。

你当地的王国

首先，我们需要为本地 JWT 身份验证令牌生成器生成我们的公钥和私钥。 为此，您可以使用此在线工具。 注意力！ 不要在任何生产环境中使用这些密钥。

之后，将两个密钥保存到适当的文件中，例如private.key和public.key ，因为您稍后将需要它们。

要为具有不同角色的不同用户生成令牌，让我们创建一个简单的node.js脚本：

 mkdir testToken ; cd testToken ; npm init -y ; npm install jsonwebtoken

将您保存到项目中的 private.key 移动到项目内的文件夹中，即 keys 文件夹。

然后将以下代码复制到 index.js 中，并根据您的需要进行相应的更改。

 const fs = require ( ' fs ' ) const jwt = require ( ' jsonwebtoken ' ) const path = require ( ' path ' ) const privateKey = fs . readFileSync ( path . join ( __dirname , ' . ' , ' keys ' , ' private.key ' )) function forUser ( email , role ) { const keyid = ' booster ' const issuer = ' booster ' return jwt . sign ( { id : email , demoRole : role , email , }, privateKey , { algorithm : ' RS256 ' , subject : email , issuer , keyid , expiresIn : 0 } ) } console . log ( ' Here is your admin user token: ' , forUser ( ' test@boostercloud.com ' , ' Admin ' ))

上面的代码是不言自明的，但基本上它使用jsonwebtoken库用私钥签署令牌，并在demoRole属性中添加一些数据，如电子邮件和角色。

最后让我们运行它：

 node index.js Here is your admin user token: eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCIsImtpZCI6ImJvb3N0ZXIifQ.eyJpZCI6InRlc3RAYm9vc3RlcmNsb3VkLmNvbSIsImRlbW9Sb2xlIjoiQWRtaW4iLCJlbWFpbCI6InRlc3RAYm9vc3RlcmNsb3VkLmNvbSIsImlhdCI6MTY1MjE5MDE1NiwiZXhwIjoxNjUyMTkwMTU2LCJpc3MiOiJib29zdGVyIiwic3ViIjoidGVzdEBib29zdGVyY2xvdWQuY29tIn0.HMBm_2MPVA_QHKr5hMTW_zmH9BFC5TplOOfSD34NrUUONzOU-1d6gKgNNRV_NX6Nem_4yksnUV64IhhLffmRNljBtIGQ-HaiVQ9S4MnNqyJCQRCArkK4xyu5EQd7RTNtLPS_xetn8kAJYzIlnO1KRNeQphplaeyEMCS5irjR9-A

呜呼！ 您已经创建了一个有效的 JWT 令牌以在 Booster 中使用。

为了在 Booster 中使用该令牌，在 API 请求标头中，您需要在令牌验证器中配置public.key ：

 Booster . configure ( ' production ' , ( config : BoosterConfig ): void => { config . tokenVerifiers = [ { publicKey : fs . readFileSync ( path . join ( __dirname , ' . ' , ' keys ' , ' public.key ' )), issuer : ' booster ' , rolesClaim : ' demoRole ' , } ] })

上面的代码将使用publicKey属性而不是jwksUri ，因为我们没有许多提供商提供的带有密钥的公共 URL。

考虑到我们使用的是我们用来登录令牌的同一发行者。

额外，额外！

一些用户询问令牌验证，该验证将检查令牌内编码的其他数据，并基于此授予或拒绝访问。 为此，Booster 配置有一个名为extraValidation的属性函数，它接收解码的令牌作为参数，允许用户执行以下操作：

 Booster . configure ( ' production ' , ( config : BoosterConfig ): void => { config . tokenVerifiers = [ { publicKey : fs . readFileSync ( path . join ( __dirname , ' . ' , ' keys ' , ' public.key ' )), issuer : ' booster ' , rolesClaim : ' demoRole ' , extraValidation : ( decodedToken ) => { if ( ! decodedToken . payload . trust ) { throw ' We don ' t trust on you ' } } } ] })

结论

如您所见，由于 JWS 被广泛采用，Booster 提供了一种基于用户角色来验证请求的标准且简单的方法，该方法将涵盖大多数常见用例。 对于那些不符合默认设置的用例，由于使用了火箭，Booster 提供了一种扩展框架的方法，但那是另一回事了。 如果您想了解更多关于如何创建火箭的信息，请参阅官方文档。

最后但同样重要的是，如果您对 Booster 或任何其他相关主题有任何疑问，我们很乐意在我们的社区频道上听到您的想法。



原文:

After several successful projects using The Booster Framework, with a variety of clients and internal projects, a common recurring question is How does Booster handle Authorization?
In this article, I will shed some light on this topic, by dissecting Booster’s approach towards authorization.

Standards, standards, standards

By design, The Booster Framework is a role-based authorized framework, which means that commands and read operations, are performed by specific roles created by the user, i.e. Admin, Customer, User, you name it. If you want to keep your API open, you can use the predefined all role, but that would be a bad idea. A better practice would be to limit the access to your API. If you want to know more about how to define roles in Booster, click here.

One widely adopted and standard way of transmitting secure information and authorizing users is the usage of JWT Tokens.

In this article, I don't want to dig deeper into the details of the JWT standard, but long story short, you will need a JWT auth provider, or create your own, which generates valid tokens for your users. Inside of those tokens includes the user roles you need as claims in your Booster application.

In the early stages of Booster development, and since we were using AWS as our first cloud provider, Booster offered endpoints to create, update, and login users, using AWS Cognito. When you created a user, you had to specify that user's role as a part of the associated information. When the user signed in, Cognito returned a JWT token with all the information associated with the user, including the role.

Then, you had to use that token in the Authorization header in your API requests as a bearer token, and Booster internally called Cognito again to verify if the token was valid, and if it contained the right role to perform the API call.

That approach worked for a while, until some Booster users didn't want to use Cognito. Some wanted to use their auth, while others were using a different auth provider like Auth0, Firebase, Cognito, Okta, or whatever.

Once again the standards to the rescue. Even if we were using JWT, Booster was tightly coupled to Cognito to verify the token and get the information associated with it. We decided to extract that part and use a standard token verification inside the Booster core, which works with the JWT tokens, no matter which provider you are using.

Side note: We removed the Cognito dependency from Booster, but if you still want to use it, you can include the AWS Auth Rocket which provides the most common Cognito features out-of-the-box for Booster applications.

Token verifiers

The JWT standard works by signing tokens using private and public key pairs using asymmetric cryptography and different algorithms (mostly RSA or ECDSA). So basically, the public key is well known by the clients, but the private key must be secret and will only be available on the server side.

Ok...but what do I need to use an auth in Booster?

In Booster you will need to specify token verifiers. You can use more than one depending on the use case, but for the moment, let's focus on using one:

Booster.configure('production', (config: BoosterConfig): void => { config.tokenVerifiers = [ { jwksUri: 'https://demoapp.firebase.com/.well-known/jwks.json', issuer: 'https://securetoken.google.com/demoapp', rolesClaim: 'firebase:groups', } ]
})

In the example above, we are using Firebase as a provider for a demo app. The jwksUri property contains the public URL where Firebase exposes the public keys as JSON web keys, the issuer specifies who is emitting tokens, and the rolesClaim value is the claim where Firebase adds the roles.

Let's create a configuration using the Cognito provider:

Booster.configure('production', (config: BoosterConfig): void => { config.tokenVerifiers = [ { jwksUri: 'https://cognito-idp.{region}.amazonaws.com/{userPoolId}/.well-known/jwks.json', issuer: 'https://cognito-idp.{region}.amazonaws.com/{userPoolId}', rolesClaim: 'cognito:groups', } ]
})

The same here, nothing weird, except provider specific configuration.

That's great, but what if I want to test my roles as a part of the development process and I don't want to stick without any auth provider.

In Booster you can use the awesome local provider for testing purposes and configure it alongside a local token verifier.

Your local kingdom

First, we need to generate our public and private keys for our local JWT auth token generator. For that, you can use this online tool. Attention! Don't use those keys in any production environment.

After that, save both keys into proper files, like private.key and public.key since you will need them later on.

To generate tokens for different users, with different roles, let’s create a simple node.js script:

mkdir testToken;cd testToken;npm init -y;npm install jsonwebtoken

Move the private.key you saved into the project into a folder inside your project i.e. keys folder.

Then copy the following code into the index.js and change it accordingly, depending on your needs.

const fs = require('fs')
const jwt = require('jsonwebtoken')
const path = require('path') const privateKey = fs.readFileSync(path.join(__dirname, '.', 'keys', 'private.key')) function forUser(email, role) { const keyid = 'booster' const issuer = 'booster' return jwt.sign( { id: email, demoRole: role, email, }, privateKey, { algorithm: 'RS256', subject: email, issuer, keyid, expiresIn: 0 } )
} console.log('Here is your admin user token: ', forUser('test@boostercloud.com', 'Admin'))

The code above is self explanatory, but basically it’s using the jsonwebtoken library to sign tokens with the private key and it’s adding some data like the email and the role in the demoRole property.

Finally let's run it:

node index.js
Here is your admin user token: eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCIsImtpZCI6ImJvb3N0ZXIifQ.eyJpZCI6InRlc3RAYm9vc3RlcmNsb3VkLmNvbSIsImRlbW9Sb2xlIjoiQWRtaW4iLCJlbWFpbCI6InRlc3RAYm9vc3RlcmNsb3VkLmNvbSIsImlhdCI6MTY1MjE5MDE1NiwiZXhwIjoxNjUyMTkwMTU2LCJpc3MiOiJib29zdGVyIiwic3ViIjoidGVzdEBib29zdGVyY2xvdWQuY29tIn0.HMBm_2MPVA_QHKr5hMTW_zmH9BFC5TplOOfSD34NrUUONzOU-1d6gKgNNRV_NX6Nem_4yksnUV64IhhLffmRNljBtIGQ-HaiVQ9S4MnNqyJCQRCArkK4xyu5EQd7RTNtLPS_xetn8kAJYzIlnO1KRNeQphplaeyEMCS5irjR9-A

Woohoo! you have created a valid JWT token to use in Booster.

In order to use that token in Booster, in the API request headers you will need to config the public.key in your token verifier:

Booster.configure('production', (config: BoosterConfig): void => { config.tokenVerifiers = [ { publicKey: fs.readFileSync(path.join(__dirname, '.', 'keys', 'public.key')), issuer: 'booster', rolesClaim: 'demoRole', } ]
})

The code above will use the publicKey property instead of the jwksUri since we don’t have a public URL with the keys, as many providers offer.

Take into account that we are using the same issuer we used to sign in the token.

Extra, extra!

Some users asked about token validations that will check other data encoded inside the token, and grant or deny access based on that. For this purpose, Booster config has a property function called extraValidation which receives the decoded token as a parameter, allowing the users to do things like this:

Booster.configure('production', (config: BoosterConfig): void => { config.tokenVerifiers = [ { publicKey: fs.readFileSync(path.join(__dirname, '.', 'keys', 'public.key')), issuer: 'booster', rolesClaim: 'demoRole', extraValidation: (decodedToken) => { if (!decodedToken.payload.trust) { throw 'We don't trust on you' } } } ]
})

Conclusions

As you have seen, Booster provides a standard and easy way of authenticating requests based on user roles which will cover most of the common use cases, since the JWS is widely adopted. For those use cases that won’t fit with the defaults, Booster provides a way of extending the framework thanks to the usage of rockets, but that’s another story. If you want to know more about how to create rockets, please refer to the official documentation.

Last but not least, if you have any questions about Booster or any other topic related, we would be happy to hear your thoughts on our community channel.
item.author: charlietfe

item.title: HRMP：基础 | HRMP: Foundation
item.description:
这些信息是什么？

您在这里看到的是一个具有公共 vec 字段和泛型类型Call的元组结构

问题

  • 如果在运行时升级的情况下接收者链的格式发生变化，会发生什么？

发送XCM的流程是什么

为此，我们将模拟由平行链发起的中继链上的简单资产转移，我们将使用Acala的shawn开发的xcm-simulator

第1步

克隆 Polkadot 存储库并构建

我通常会在这一点上切换到一个新的分支，这样我就不会意外地将任何东西推到 master 上。

第2步

转到 xcm-executor 库文件并将这些日志添加到process_instruction函数中，这样我们就可以看到在进行简单传输时会发生什么。

 ... /// Process a single XCM instruction, mutating the state of the XCM virtual machine. fn process_instruction ( & mut self , instr : Instruction < Config :: Call > ) -> Result < (), XcmError > { println! ( "instruction: {:?}" , instr ); println! ( "origin: {:?}" , self .origin ); println! ( "holding before: {:?}" , self .holding ); let result = match instr { WithdrawAsset ( assets ) => { // Take `assets` from the origin account (on-chain) and place in holding. let origin = self .origin .as_ref () .ok_or ( XcmError :: BadOrigin ) ? ; for asset in assets .drain () .into_iter () { Config :: AssetTransactor :: withdraw_asset ( & asset , origin ) ? ; self .holding .subsume ( asset ); } Ok (()) }, ... }; println! ( "holding after: {:?}" , self .holding ); result ...

此外，现在所有不熟悉的内容将在下一部分变得更加清晰，我相信在深入研究细节之前给出一个高层次的概述会有所帮助，所以现在就继续进行下去，我希望最终值得你花时间。

 running 1 test instruction: WithdrawAsset ( MultiAssets ([ MultiAsset { id : Concrete ( MultiLocation { parents: 0, interior: Here }) , fun: Fungible ( 10 ) }])) origin: Some ( MultiLocation { parents: 0, interior: X1 ( Parachain ( 1 )) }) holding before: Assets { fungible: {} , non_fungible: {} } holding after: Assets { fungible: { Concrete ( MultiLocation { parents: 0, interior: Here }) : 10 } , non_fungible: {} } instruction: BuyExecution { fees: MultiAsset { id : Concrete ( MultiLocation { parents: 0, interior: Here }) , fun: Fungible ( 10 ) } , weight_limit: Unlimited } origin: Some ( MultiLocation { parents: 0, interior: X1 ( Parachain ( 1 )) }) holding before: Assets { fungible: { Concrete ( MultiLocation { parents: 0, interior: Here }) : 10 } , non_fungible: {} } holding after: Assets { fungible: { Concrete ( MultiLocation { parents: 0, interior: Here }) : 10 } , non_fungible: {} } instruction: DepositAsset { assets: Wild ( All ) , max_assets: 1, beneficiary: MultiLocation { parents: 0, interior: X1 ( Parachain ( 2 )) } } origin: Some ( MultiLocation { parents: 0, interior: X1 ( Parachain ( 1 )) }) holding before: Assets { fungible: { Concrete ( MultiLocation { parents: 0, interior: Here }) : 10 } , non_fungible: {} } holding after: Assets { fungible: {} , non_fungible: {} } test tests::withdraw_and_deposit ... ok


原文:

HRMP - Horizontal Relay Message Passing is the light weight version of XCMP - Cross-Chain Message Passing that does not allow parachains to directly send messages to each other but instead uses the relay chain to facilitate the passing of messages between parachains via a combination of UMP - Upward Message Passing & DMP - Downward Message Passing protocols.

What are these messages?

Polkadot defines this generic cross-consensus-messages format called XCM which is designed to be platform agnostic, what that means is you can not only send messages from blockchain to blockchain but also to smart contracts of other chains.

XCM is defined as

pub struct Xcm<Call>(pub Vec<Instruction<Call>>);

What you see here is a tuple struct with a public vec field and the generic type Call

Inside the Vec is a set of instructions that a specific consensus system must define to have the recipient consensus system execute in a step wise manner, what that means is these set of instructions a.k.a Instruction<Call> is an enum with variants of standard instructions/dispatchable calls that exists in most substrate based chains(i.e. TransferAsset) and a non-standard instruction Transact which allows calls that are specific to the recipient chain.

Questions

  • What happens when the formatting of the recipient chain changes in the event of a runtime upgrade?

XCM acts as a layer before the actual runtime of your chain, if you have an instruction to WithdrawAsset/TransferAsset then you can just program the abstraction allowing XCM stay consistent but under the hood implement what ever changes to the formatting and/or pallet you desire, this only applies to standard instructions, for the Transact instruction you have to make sure the transaction format(i.e. dispatchable function format) of the recipient chain stays the same during the cross-chain-communication.

  • What's the process of sending XCM

For this purpose we are going to simulate a simple asset transfer on the relay chain initiated by a parachain, we'll be using the xcm-simulator developed by shawn from Acala

Step 1

Clone the Polkadot repo and build

i usually switch to a new branch at this point just so i don't push anything to master by accident.

Step 2

Head over to the xcm-executor library file and add these logs in the process_instruction function just so we can see what happens when we do a simple transfer.

... /// Process a single XCM instruction, mutating the state of the XCM virtual machine. fn process_instruction(&mut self, instr: Instruction<Config::Call>) -> Result<(), XcmError> { println!("instruction: {:?}", instr); println!("origin: {:?}", self.origin); println!("holding before: {:?}", self.holding); let result = match instr { WithdrawAsset(assets) => { // Take `assets` from the origin account (on-chain) and place in holding. let origin = self.origin.as_ref().ok_or(XcmError::BadOrigin)?; for asset in assets.drain().into_iter() { Config::AssetTransactor::withdraw_asset(&asset, origin)?; self.holding.subsume(asset); } Ok(()) }, ... }; println!("holding after: {:?}", self.holding); result
...

Also all that is unfamiliar right now will become clearer in the next part, I believe giving a high level overview before delving into the nitty gritty helps, so for now just follow along and I hope it will be worth you time in the end.

running 1 test
instruction: WithdrawAsset(MultiAssets([MultiAsset { id: Concrete(MultiLocation { parents: 0, interior: Here }), fun: Fungible(10) }]))
origin: Some(MultiLocation { parents: 0, interior: X1(Parachain(1)) })
holding before: Assets { fungible: {}, non_fungible: {} }
holding after: Assets { fungible: {Concrete(MultiLocation { parents: 0, interior: Here }): 10}, non_fungible: {} }
instruction: BuyExecution { fees: MultiAsset { id: Concrete(MultiLocation { parents: 0, interior: Here }), fun: Fungible(10) }, weight_limit: Unlimited }
origin: Some(MultiLocation { parents: 0, interior: X1(Parachain(1)) })
holding before: Assets { fungible: {Concrete(MultiLocation { parents: 0, interior: Here }): 10}, non_fungible: {} }
holding after: Assets { fungible: {Concrete(MultiLocation { parents: 0, interior: Here }): 10}, non_fungible: {} }
instruction: DepositAsset { assets: Wild(All), max_assets: 1, beneficiary: MultiLocation { parents: 0, interior: X1(Parachain(2)) } }
origin: Some(MultiLocation { parents: 0, interior: X1(Parachain(1)) })
holding before: Assets { fungible: {Concrete(MultiLocation { parents: 0, interior: Here }): 10}, non_fungible: {} }
holding after: Assets { fungible: {}, non_fungible: {} }
test tests::withdraw_and_deposit ... ok
item.author: doordashcon

item.title: 如何使用纯 javascript 通过 url 创建文件下载器 | How to create a file downloader via url using pure javascript
item.description:
朋友们好，今天在这个博客中，您将学习如何使用纯 Javascript 通过 URL 创建文件下载器。 在我们之前的博客中，我们看到了如何使用 HTML、CSS 和 Sortable JS 创建拖放或可排序列表。 之前分享了很多javascript相关的项目，大家可以去看看，别忘了去看看HTML、CSS、Javascript项目。

此工具（通过 URL 的文件下载器）可以下载任何文件，如图像、视频、pdf、SVG 等。用户必须粘贴文件的有效 URL 并单击下载按钮才能下载文件。 请记住，该文件应该可以公开下载。

起初，我得到了用户输入的文件 URL，然后使用 fetch() API 获取了文件。 收到响应后，我将响应作为 blob() 返回，在另一个 then 方法中，我得到一个包含文件详细信息的对象。

你可能会喜欢这些：

然后，使用URL.createObjectURL()方法，我创建了该文件对象的 URL。 此 URL 存储在文档窗口中。 最后，我创建了一个<a>标记并将 URL 存储为该标记的 href 值，然后单击它以下载文件。 观看上面的视频，了解每个 JavaScript 行的详细说明。

注意：如果您在文件下载期间在控制台中收到 cors（跨域资源共享）错误，这意味着浏览器阻止了请求，因为请求的站点不允许您访问该文件。

这个文件下载器是用纯 JavaScript 制作的，没有使用服务器端语言来创建它。 如果你喜欢它并想创建所以你可以查看源代码或预览。

预览版可在此处获得。

HTML Code HTML 代码

<!DOCTYPE html> <html lang="en"> <head> <meta charset="UTF-8"> <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0"> <title>Download Any File By URL Using Javascript - InCoder</title> <link rel="stylesheet" href="main.css"> <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/font-awesome/6.1.1/css/all.min.css"> </head> <body> <div class="container"> <div class="title">File Downloader via URL</div> <p>Paste url of any image, video or PDF to Download. This tool is made with pure Javascript</p> <form id="fileDownloader"> <div class="formInput"> <i class="fa-solid fa-link"></i> <input type="text" id="fileURL" placeholder="Paste the URL" oninvalid="this.setCustomValidity('Enter a valid URL')" required /> </div> <button type="submit">Download</button> </form> </div> <script src="script.js"></script> </body> </html>

CSS Code CSS 代码

/* ------------------------ Created By InCoder ------------------------ */ @import url("https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Poppins:ital,wght@0,100;0,200;0,300;0,400;0,500;0,600;0,700;0,800;0,900;1,100;1,200;1,300;1,400;1,500;1,600;1,700;1,800;1,900&display=swap"); * { margin: 0; padding: 0; box-sizing: border-box; font-family: "Poppins", sans-serif; } body { height: 100vh; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; background-color: #015d52; } .container { padding: 0.5rem; border-radius: 0.5rem; background-color: #fff; width: clamp(18rem, 45vw, 25rem); } .container .title { font-weight: 600; color: #202020; text-align: center; font-size: clamp(1rem, 4vw, 1.6rem); } .container p { font-size: 0.9rem; } .container button[type="submit"] { border: 0; width: 100%; opacity: 0.9; color: #fff; height: 2.5rem; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1rem; margin-top: 0.8rem; border-radius: 0.3rem; background-color: #015d52; transition: opacity 0.2s ease-in-out; } .container button[type="submit"]:hover { opacity: 1; } .formInput input { border: 0; width: 100%; height: 100%; margin-left: .3rem; } .formInput input::placeholder { color: #4e4e4ed4; } .formInput { margin-top: .8rem; width: 100%; display: flex; height: 2.5rem; padding: .2rem; color: #4e4e4ed4; align-items: center; border-radius: .3rem; border: 2px solid #4e4e4ed4; } .formInput input:focus { outline: none; }

Javascript Code Javascript代码

let form = document.querySelector('#fileDownloader') downloadBtn = form.querySelector('button') form.addEventListener('submit', e => { e.preventDefault() let inputURL = e.target.querySelector('#fileURL').value.trim() downloadBtn.innerText = 'Downloading File Please Wait....' downloadBtn.setAttribute('disabled', 'disabled') getFile(inputURL) }) const getFile = (url) => { fetch(url).then(response => response.blob()).then(file => { let tempLink = URL.createObjectURL(file) let anchorTag = document.createElement('a') anchorTag.href = tempLink anchorTag.download = url.replace(/^.*[\\\/]/, '') document.body.appendChild(anchorTag) anchorTag.click() downloadBtn.innerText = 'Download File' downloadBtn.removeAttribute('disabled', 'disabled') URL.revokeObjectURL(tempLink); anchorTag.remove(); }).catch(() => { alert("Unable to download file!"); downloadBtn.innerText = "Download File"; downloadBtn.removeAttribute('disabled', 'disabled') }) }


原文:

Hello friends, today in this blog you will learn how to create a file downloader via URL using pure Javascript. In our previous blog, we saw how to create drag & drop or sortable lists using HTML, CSS, and Sortable JS. Earlier, I shared many projects related to javascript, you can check if you want, and don't forget to check HTML, CSS, and Javascript projects.

This tool (File Downloader via URL) can download any file like image, video, pdf, SVG, etc. Users have to paste a valid URL of the file and click the download button to download the file. Remember, the file should be publicly accessible to download.

At first, I got the user entered file URL, and using fetch() API, I fetched the file. Once I got a response, I return the response as a blob() and in another then method, I got an object that contains details of the file.

You may like these:

Then, using URL.createObjectURL() method, I created a URL of that file object. This URL is stored in the document window. At last, I created a <a> tag and stored the URL as the href value of this tag, and click it so the file download. Watch the above video for a detailed explanation of each JavaScript line.

Note: If you get a cors (cross-origin resource sharing) error in the console during file downloading, that means the browser blocked the request because the requested site doesn’t allow you to access that file.

This file downloader is made with pure JavaScript no server-side language is used to create it. If you liked it and want to create So you can check the source code or preview.

The Preview is available here.

HTML Code 

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>Download Any File By URL Using Javascript - InCoder</title>
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="main.css">
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/font-awesome/6.1.1/css/all.min.css">
</head>
<body>
    <div class="container">
        <div class="title">File Downloader via URL</div>
        <p>Paste url of any image, video or PDF to Download. This tool is made with pure Javascript</p>
        <form id="fileDownloader">
            <div class="formInput">
                <i class="fa-solid fa-link"></i>
                <input type="text" id="fileURL" placeholder="Paste the URL" oninvalid="this.setCustomValidity('Enter a valid URL')" required />
            </div>
            <button type="submit">Download</button>
        </form>
    </div>

    <script src="script.js"></script>
</body>
</html>

CSS Code 

/* ------------------------ Created By InCoder ------------------------ */

@import url("https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Poppins:ital,wght@0,100;0,200;0,300;0,400;0,500;0,600;0,700;0,800;0,900;1,100;1,200;1,300;1,400;1,500;1,600;1,700;1,800;1,900&display=swap");

* {
  margin: 0;
  padding: 0;
  box-sizing: border-box;
  font-family: "Poppins", sans-serif;
}

body {
  height: 100vh;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  background-color: #015d52;
}

.container {
  padding: 0.5rem;
  border-radius: 0.5rem;
  background-color: #fff;
  width: clamp(18rem, 45vw, 25rem);
}

.container .title {
  font-weight: 600;
  color: #202020;
  text-align: center;
  font-size: clamp(1rem, 4vw, 1.6rem);
}

.container p {
  font-size: 0.9rem;
}

.container button[type="submit"] {
  border: 0;
  width: 100%;
  opacity: 0.9;
  color: #fff;
  height: 2.5rem;
  cursor: pointer;
  font-size: 1rem;
  margin-top: 0.8rem;
  border-radius: 0.3rem;
  background-color: #015d52;
  transition: opacity 0.2s ease-in-out;
}

.container button[type="submit"]:hover {
  opacity: 1;
}

.formInput input {
    border: 0;
    width: 100%;
    height: 100%;
    margin-left: .3rem;
}

.formInput input::placeholder {
    color: #4e4e4ed4;
}

.formInput {
  margin-top: .8rem;
  width: 100%;
  display: flex;
  height: 2.5rem;
  padding: .2rem;
  color: #4e4e4ed4;
  align-items: center;
  border-radius: .3rem;
  border: 2px solid #4e4e4ed4;
}

.formInput input:focus {
    outline: none;
}

Javascript Code 

let form = document.querySelector('#fileDownloader')
downloadBtn = form.querySelector('button')

form.addEventListener('submit', e => {
    e.preventDefault()
    let inputURL = e.target.querySelector('#fileURL').value.trim()
    downloadBtn.innerText = 'Downloading File Please Wait....'
    downloadBtn.setAttribute('disabled', 'disabled')
    getFile(inputURL)
})

const getFile = (url) => {
    fetch(url).then(response => response.blob()).then(file => {
        let tempLink = URL.createObjectURL(file)
        let anchorTag = document.createElement('a')
        anchorTag.href = tempLink
        anchorTag.download = url.replace(/^.*[\\\/]/, '')
        document.body.appendChild(anchorTag)
        anchorTag.click()
        downloadBtn.innerText = 'Download File'
        downloadBtn.removeAttribute('disabled', 'disabled')
        URL.revokeObjectURL(tempLink);
        anchorTag.remove();
    }).catch(() => {
        alert("Unable to download file!");
        downloadBtn.innerText = "Download File";
        downloadBtn.removeAttribute('disabled', 'disabled')
    })
}
item.author: incoderweb

item.title: ✅ 5 个用 Jetpack Compose 制作的超棒的 Android 按钮样式 | ✅5 Awesome Android Button Styles Made with Jetpack Compose
item.description:
以下是使用 Android Jetpack Compose 制作的按钮。

这些是带有渐变背景的按钮。 它们是使用Brush API 实现的。 我已经在所有这些上添加了onClick 。 预建的Button()不支持渐变背景。 所以，我用Box() API 制作了它们。

源代码：渐变按钮

它有 3 种状态 - 播放、暂停和加载。 最初，它显示播放图标。 当您点击它时，播放图标将变为加载栏。 在这种状态下，您可以从服务器下载歌曲（使用 ViewModel）。 下载完成后，加载栏将变为暂停图标。 这是您可以播放歌曲的地方。

源代码：音乐按钮

我喜欢这个设计。 按钮有不同的角尺寸。 感谢RoundedCornerShape()方法提供了topEnd 、 bottomStart等参数...它们允许我们为每个角设置不同的大小。

源代码： Jetpack Compose 按钮​​样式

当您点击按钮时，其比例值将从 1f 变为 0.9f 再到 1f。 同样的事情也发生在心形图标上。 这是一种“类似动画”的效果。

源代码：带有 onClick 动画的按钮

源代码：渐变边框按钮

谢谢阅读。 如果您有任何建议或疑问，请在评论中告诉我。



原文:

Here are the Buttons made with Android Jetpack Compose.

1. Gradient Buttons:


These are the buttons with gradient backgrounds. They are implemented using the Brush API. I have added onClick on all of them. The pre-built Button() doesn't support gradient background. So, I made them with Box() API.

Source Code: Gradient Buttons

2. Music Button:


It has 3 states - play, pause, and loading. Initially, it shows the play icon. When you tap on it, the play icon will be changed to the loading bar. In this state, you can download the song from the server (using the ViewModel). When the download is completed, the loading bar will be changed to the pause icon. This is where you can play the song.

Source Code: Music Button

3. Buttons with Different Corner Sizes:


I like this design. The buttons have different corner sizes. Thanks to RoundedCornerShape() method for providing the arguments like topEnd, bottomStart, etc... They allow us to set different sizes for each corner.

Source Code: Jetpack Compose Button Style

4. Button with onClick Animation:


When you tap on the button, its scale value will be animated from 1f to 0.9f to 1f. The same thing happens to the heart icon too. It is a "like animation" effect.

Source Code: Button with onClick Animation

5. Buttons with Gradient Border:

Source Code: Gradient Border Button

Thanks for reading. If you have any suggestions or doubts, let me know in the comments.
item.author: coder_kumar

item.title: 5 个常见的 Amazon Kinesis 问题 | 5 Common Amazon Kinesis Issues
item.description:
无论您需要处理视频、音频还是物联网流数据，Kinesis 都是您的最佳选择。

通常，当 SQS 或 SNS 太低级时，您会使用它。

但与 AWS 上的所有其他服务一样，Kinesis 是一种专业工具，但也有其复杂性。 本文将讨论最常见的问题并解释如何解决这些问题。 所以，让我们开始吧！

如果您的 Kinesis 流只有一个分片，即使流中正在等待多条记录，也不会并行调用 Lambda 函数。 要扩展到大量并行调用，您需要向 Kinesis Stream 添加更多分片。

这是控制并行 Lambda 调用的一个很好的功能。 但如果函数执行时间过长，它会减慢整体处理速度。

如果您不依赖以前的事件，您可以使用更多的分片，Lambda 会自动扩展到更多的并发调用。 但请记住，Lambda 本身对 1,000 个并发调用有软限制。 您可以联系 AWS 以取消此限制。 在此之上没有明确定义的硬限制，但 AWS 提到了 10,000 的倍数。

如果您在循环中调用 put_record 以将记录从 Lambda 函数发布到 Kinesis 流，这可能会在循环中失败。 要解决此问题，请确保捕获 put_record 方法抛出的任何错误； 否则，您的函数将崩溃并且仅部分发布记录列表。

如果一个 Lambda 调用负责将多条记录发布到 Kinesis 流，您必须确保 Lambda 函数的崩溃不会丢失数据。 根据您的使用案例，这可能意味着您需要在 Lambda 函数前使用重试或其他队列。

您还可以尝试捕获任何错误而不是崩溃，然后将丢失的记录放在其他地方以确保它们不会丢失。

您尝试将记录从 Kinesis Firehose 推送到 Lambda 函数，但出现错误。 这通常是 IAM 角色的权限问题。 要解决此问题，请确保为您的 firehose 分配正确的 IAM 角色。

在策略文档的资源部分，您需要确保列出所有 Lambda 函数的 ARN。 您可以使用 ARN 中的通配符或 ARN 数组来实现此目的。

但是可能有许多其他权限问题阻止调用。 他们之中有一些是：

  • 缺少“动作”：[“lambda:InvokeFunction”]
  • 有“效果”：在某处“拒绝”
  • 将错误的角色分配给消防软管

您尝试在给定时间段内过于频繁地更新分片计数。 为了解决这个问题，您可以调用其他函数，如SplitShard和MergeShards ，具有更慷慨的配额。

通常，您不知道有多少分片足以处理您的负载，因此您必须随着时间的推移更新它们的数量。 要在此处引用文档，您不能

  • 每个流的每个滚动 24 小时周期可扩展十倍以上
  • 将流的当前分片数扩展到两倍以上
  • 将流的当前分片数缩小到一半以下
  • 在一个流中扩展到超过 10000 个分片
  • 缩小超过 10000 个分片的流，除非结果小于 10000 个分片
  • 扩展到超过您帐户的分片限制

如果您使用其他方法，则可以绕过一些限制，从而为您在分片方面提供更大的灵活性。

您在创建新流后过早地进行了交互。 创建新流最多可能需要 10 分钟才能完成。 您可以在创建流后设置超时，或确保重试几次以解决此问题。

创建新的流或分片不是即时操作。 它发生得非常快，但在最坏的情况下您可能需要等待几分钟。 与任何分布式系统一样，您必须牢记延迟。 否则，您的日志将充满错误。

概括

如果您必须实时处理数据或媒体，最好选择Kinesis on AWS 。

遗憾的是，它不像 SQS 和 SNS 那样简单，但也比那些服务更灵活。

您最好的做法是了解服务的局限性，这样您就不会被可避免的错误消息所困扰。 此外，请确保对您的 Lambda 函数进行稳健的编程，这样它们就不会因为一半的数据尚未处理而崩溃。

使用 Dashbird 监控 Kinesis

此外，Dashbird 将根据Well-Architected Framework评估您的所有 Kinesis 日志。 因此，不仅仅是指标和错误，而是可操作的信息，可通过 AWS 最佳实践改进您的架构。

立即免费试用 Dashbird ，或查看我们的产品之旅！

在 Dashbird，我们了解无服务器的核心理念和价值是专注于客户和避免繁重工作的能力。 这正是我们提供的。 最后，我们允许开发人员*再次考虑最终用户*，而不会因调试和警报管理而分心，也不会担心某些东西是否正常工作。

进一步阅读：



原文:

Amazon Kinesis is the real-time stream processing service of AWS. Whether you got video, audio, or IoT streaming data to handle, Kinesis is the way to go.

Kinesis is a serverless managed service that integrates nicely with other services like Lambda or S3. Often, you will use it when SQS or SNS is too low-level.  

But as with all the other services on AWS, Kinesis is a professional tool that comes with its share of complications. This article will discuss the most common issues and explain how to fix them. So, let's get going!

1. What Limits Apply when AWS Lambda is Subscribed to a Kinesis Stream?

If your Kinesis stream only has one shard, the Lambda function won't be called in parallel even if multiple records are waiting in the stream. To scale up to numerous parallel invocations, you need to add more shards to a Kinesis Stream.

Kinesis will strictly serialize all your invocations per shard. This is a nice feature for controlling your parallel Lambda invocations. But it can slow down overall processing if the function takes too long to execute.

If you aren't relying on previous events, you can use more shards, and Lambda will automatically scale up to more concurrent invocations. But keep in mind that Lambda itself has a soft limit on 1,000 concurrent invocations. You can reach out to AWS to get this limit lifted. There isn't an explicitly defined hard limit above that, but AWS mentions its multiples of 10,000.

2. Data Loss with Kinesis Streams and Lambda

If you call put_record in a loop to publish records from a Lambda function to a Kinesis stream, this can fail mid-loop. To fix this, make sure you catch any errors the put_record method throws; otherwise, your function will crash and only partially publish the list of records.
