item.title: 从 Lambda 函数中的 HTTP 标头中删除敏感信息 | Removing sensitive information from HTTP headers in Lambda functions
item.description:
当我们的 Lambda 函数收到错误时，我们可以通过中断 HTTP 响应来避免显示敏感的标头信息。 我们可以快速创建一个只返回我们需要的属性并隐藏其余属性的函数。

其中一些服务在Lambda函数上运行。 一个这样的函数使用第 3 方 API 来获取一些数据。 该应用程序使用流行的 HTTP 客户端axios发出请求，并使用JWT进行身份验证。

有一天，第 3 方 API 响应错误。 爱丽丝是 Example Corp 的一名开发人员，她查看了日志，令她惊讶的是，她看到了如下内容：

 { // lots of other properties "headers" : { "Accept" : "application/json" , "Authorzation" : "Bearer <JWT TOKEN HERE>" , "User-Agent" : "axios/0.27.2" }, "method" : "get" ,
}
// more properties

不好，爱丽丝对自己说。 日志中有敏感信息。 她很快就该问题创建了一张票，并开始从响应中删除令牌。

她想将该解决方案专门应用于应用程序向 3rd 方 API 发出的请求。 她决定将一个 axios 客户端专门用于这些调用，并使用拦截器功能从响应中删除敏感信息。

代码

她不需要做很多工作。 最后，她的代码是这样的：

 import axios from ' axios ' ; const axiosInstance = axios . create (); axiosInstance . interceptors . response . use ( ( response ) => { return response ; }, ( error ) => { const sanitizedError = { message : error . message , name : error . name , stack : error . stack , method : error . config . method , url : error . config . url , status : error . stats , }; return Promise . reject ( sanitizedError ); } ); // Lambda handler export const handler = async () => { try { const { data } = await axiosInstance . get ( ' <URL_TO_CALL> ' , { headers : { Authorization : ' Bearer TOKEN ' , }, }); // process response } catch ( error ) { console . log ( ' An error occurred ' , error ); throw error ; } };

这里我们为response实现拦截器，但request拦截器的工作方式类似。

拦截器

响应拦截器（由axios.interceptors.response.use()方法提供）停止响应并允许我们在看到结果之前实现自定义逻辑。

与Promises类似，拦截器接受两个函数参数。

当一切按预期工作时，第一个函数 ( successHandler ) 会拦截响应。 它的参数将是response对象。 在这个例子中，我们不想对响应做任何事情，所以我们将return它。

第二个参数（ errorHandler ）是修改抛出错误的函数。 中间件将error对象作为参数提供给回调函数。 我们可以创建一个具有我们想要的属性的自定义对象。 如代码片段所示，我们忽略了包含令牌的 axios config对象。 我们只保留我们想要使用的属性： name 、 message 、 method和其他几个。

现在让我们调用 Lambda 函数。 我们应该只看到我们在sanitizedError对象中设置的属性。 返回被拒绝的 Promise（或抛出错误）很重要，否则 axios 将认为响应成功。

三、总结

当一个 axios 请求抛出错误时，我们会在日志中看到整个响应对象。 响应可能包含敏感信息，例如授权令牌。 我们可以通过拦截响应并使用自定义错误对象拒绝承诺来删除这些字段。

4. References, further reading 4. 参考资料，延伸阅读

Axios Interceptors - More about axios interceptors Axios 拦截器- 关于 axios 拦截器的更多信息



原文:

We can avoid displaying sensitive header information by interrupting the HTTP response when our Lambda function receives an error. We can quickly create a function that returns only the properties we need and hides the rest.

1. The scenario

Example Corp has several microservices. Some of these services run on Lambda functions. One such function uses a 3rd party API to fetch some data. The application uses the popular HTTP client axios for making the requests, and authenticates with a JWT.

One day the 3rd party API responded with an error. Alice, who is a developer at Example Corp, looked at the logs, and to her surprise, she saw something like this:

{ // lots of other properties "headers": { "Accept": "application/json", "Authorzation": "Bearer <JWT TOKEN HERE>", "User-Agent": "axios/0.27.2" }, "method": "get",
}
// more properties

It's not good, Alice said to herself. There is sensitive information in the logs. She quickly created a ticket on the issue and started to remove the token from the response.

2. The solution

She wanted to apply the solution specifically to the requests the application made to the 3rd party API. She decided to dedicate an axios client to these calls and use an interceptor function to remove the sensitive information from the response.

2.1. Code

She didn't have to do a lot of work. In the end, her code was similar to this:

import axios from 'axios'; const axiosInstance = axios.create();
axiosInstance.interceptors.response.use( (response) => { return response; }, (error) => { const sanitizedError = { message: error.message, name: error.name, stack: error.stack, method: error.config.method, url: error.config.url, status: error.stats, }; return Promise.reject(sanitizedError); }
); // Lambda handler
export const handler = async () => { try { const { data } = await axiosInstance.get('<URL_TO_CALL>', { headers: { Authorization: 'Bearer TOKEN', }, }); // process response } catch (error) { console.log('An error occurred', error); throw error; }
};

The axios interceptor is available on both the request and response sides. Here we implement the interceptors for the response, but request interceptors work similarly.

2.2. Interceptor

Response interceptors (provided by the axios.interceptors.response.use() method) stop the response and allow us to implement custom logic before we can see the result.

Similarly to Promises the interceptor accepts two function arguments.

The first function (successHandler) intercepts the response when everything works as expected. Its argument will be the response object. In this example, we don't want to do anything with the response, so we will return it.

The second argument (errorHandler) is the function that modifies the thrown errors. The middleware provides the error object as an argument to the callback function. We can create a custom object with the properties we want. As the code snippet shows, we ignore the axios config object that contains the token. We only keep the properties that we want to use: name, message, method, and a couple of others.

Let's invoke the Lambda function now. We should only see the properties we have set in the sanitizedError object. sanitizedError doesn't contain the headers, so we won't see the Authorization header in the logs anymore.

It's important to return a rejected Promise (or throw an error), or axios will consider the response a success.

3. Summary

When an axios request throws an error, we will see the entire response object in the logs. The response might contain sensitive information, for example, authorization tokens. We can remove these fields by intercepting the response and rejecting a promise with a custom error object.

4. References, further reading

Axios Interceptors - More about axios interceptors
item.author: arpadt

item.title: 使用 terraform 创建 Azure 容器应用 | Create Azure Container Apps with terraform
item.description:
微软在 Microsoft Build 上宣布Azure 容器应用程序现已普遍可用 (GA)。

如果您不熟悉 Azure 容器应用程序 (ACA)，我建议您查看此处的文档。

完全托管的无服务器容器服务，用于大规模构建和部署现代应用程序

这就是微软自己推销产品的方式。

我认为这是一个非常有趣的平台，它提供了 Kubernetes 的一些好处，抽象了很多概念和复杂性。

为了开始使用它，我通常使用一些 terraform 代码创建一个 repo，这样我就可以启动一组资源，使用它们，然后在完成后将它们全部销毁。

您可能知道，并非所有 Azure 资源从第一天起都可以在 terraform azure provider(s) 中使用，但在一个月左右的时间里，我们有很棒的 terraform AzApi提供程序。

按照这个github 问题跟踪在azurerm官方提供程序中添加对它的支持。

多亏了这个提供者，使用 terraform 创建 Azure 容器应用程序非常容易。

以下是您配置提供程序的方式：

在这里您可以看到如何使用azapi_resource创建 Azure 容器应用程序

提示

为了发现属性，我首先在 Azure 门户中进行更改，然后运行以下az-cli命令：

az containerapp list

然后您需要将 json 转换为 terraform 等效项。

您还可以安装应提供完成支持的Terraform AzApi Provider Visual Studio Code Extension VS Code extension。

感谢 piizei在这里提出建议

如果你想看一个更高级的例子，你可以在这里查看我的 repo。

希望这对您有所帮助，如果您有任何问题/建议，请随时发表评论！



原文:

Microsoft announced at Microsoft Build that Azure Container Apps are now generally available (GA).

If you're not familiar with Azure Container Apps (ACA) I suggest you to go and check out the documentation here.

Fully managed serverless container service for building and deploying modern apps at scale

This is how Microsoft itself markets the product.

I think it's a very interesting platform and it offers some of the benefits of Kubernetes, abstracting a lot of concepts and complexity.

In order to start playing around with it, I usually create a repo with some terraform code so I can spin up a set of resources, play with them and then destroy all of them when I'm done.

As you may know not all Azure resources are available in terraform azure provider(s) from day 1, but for a month or so we have the awesome AzApi provider for terraform.

Follow this github issue that tracks adding support for it in the azurerm official provider.

The AzAPI provider enables you to manage any Azure resource type using any API version.

Thanks to this provider it is very easy to create an Azure Container App with terraform.

Here's how you configure the provider:

Here you can see how you can use azapi_resource to create an Azure Container App

Tips

In order to discover the properties, I first make the changes it in the Azure portal, then I'm running the following az-cli command:

az containerapp list

and then you need to transform the json to the terraform equivalent.

You can also install Terraform AzApi Provider Visual Studio Code Extension VS Code extension that should provide completion support.

Kudos to piizei for coming up with the suggestion here

If you want to see a more advanced example, you can give a look at my repo here.

Hope you find this helpful, if you have any question/suggestion, don't hesitate to comment!
item.author: maxx_don

item.title: 我的新 Macbook 设置 (2022) | My New Macbook Setup (2022)
item.description:
这篇文章会在我的 github 仓库https://github.com/maoxiaoke/setup-a-mac-for-frontend-dev中持续更新。

设置新的 Macbook 将是艰难而繁琐的。 每次我得到一台新的 Macbook 时，我都会按照相同的步骤来设置它以适应我的工作体验。

我创建了我的设置记录，希望它对其他人也有帮助！

立即第一步

启用点击点击

在设置新的 Macbook 时，我们所做的第一个更改是启用触控板的点击功能。

转到「系统偏好设置 -> 触控板 -> 指向 & 单击」并启用「点击以单击」选项。

启用三指拖动

通常，在您的 Macbook 上“点击以单击”以避免强行单击触控板。 但这在拖动和重新定位窗口时不起作用。

另外，我非常喜欢拖树指。

  1. 转到「系统偏好设置 -> 辅助功能」。
  2. 在辅助功能侧边栏中，选择选项「指针控制」。
  3. 点击按钮「触控板选项」。
  4. 开启拖拽，选择「三指拖拽」。

更换壁纸

访问wallhaven以找到精美的壁纸并替换默认壁纸。

下载边缘

在发现chrome 正在吃掉我旧 Mac 的电池后，我转而使用edge 。

安装自制软件

只需按照他们的安装指南，将/bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"复制到终端。

由于GFW的原因，中国用户将无法连接到 github。

只需将/bin/zsh -c "$(curl -fsSL https://gitee.com/cunkai/HomebrewCN/raw/master/Homebrew.sh)"复制到您的终端。 安装自制软件时它会做同样的事情。

设置终端

使用iTerm2作为 macOS 终端替代品。

  1. 使用 Homebrew 安装 iTerm2
 $ brew install --cask iterm2
  1. 更改 iterm2 的一些设置
  • 转到「 iterm2 Preferences -> Profiles -> Default -> Window 」，添加透明度和模糊。

  • 转到「 iterm2 Preferences -> Profiles -> Default -> Keys -> Key Mappings 」，将标准的替换为预设的“自然文本编辑”。

这样做的好处是您可以自定义 shell 并添加插件。 与旧的 Bash shell 不同，旧的 Bash shell 看起来不吸引人，并且会使您难以使用。

 $ sh -c " $( curl -fsSL https://raw.github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/master/tools/install.sh ) "
  1. 选择你最喜欢的主题

您可以选择多个主题。 我最喜欢的主题是honukai ，它应该看起来像屏幕截图中的样子：

如需完整指南，请深入了解它的文档。

  1. 添加有用的 oh-my-zsh 插件

我将分享我的前 3 个插件。

 plugins=( git autojump zsh-syntax-highlighting )

默认情况下启用git插件。 它提供了许多别名和一些有用的功能。

要使用它，请将git添加到 zshrc 文件中的 plugins 数组中：

使用 Homebrew 安装autojump 。

之后，将自动跳转添加到您的autojump文件中：

  1. 但值得付出努力。

    克隆 oh-my-zsh 的插件目录中的zsh-syntax-highlighting存储库：

     $ git clone https://github.com/zsh-users/zsh-syntax-highlighting.git ${ ZSH_CUSTOM :- ~/.oh-my-zsh/custom } /plugins/zsh-syntax-highlighting

    然后，激活~/.zshrc中的插件：

     plugins=(... zsh-syntax-highlighting)

    将插件添加到.zshrc文件后，您必须使用命令source ~/.zshrc重新启动 zsh 以激活它们。

    你可以在这里找到我所有的.zshrc设置。

    安装阿尔弗雷德

    现在，是Alfred的时候了——Spotlight 的替代品。

    为 Alfred 使用 Spotlight 键盘快捷键

    安装 Alfred 时，我做的第一件事是禁用 Spotlight 的默认快捷方式并将其用于 Alfred。

    为此，请打开System Preference -> Keyboard -> Shortcuts 。 单击“Spotlight”并禁用“显示 Spotlight 搜索”。

    打开 Alfred 设置并使用释放的⌘ Space组合启动 Alfred。

    剪贴板历史

    复制和粘贴是开发人员每天都会做的事情。 我唯一要做的就是将快捷方式重新映射到⌘ . 到剪贴板历史记录。

    工作流程

    我没有安装很多工作流程。 我安装的工作流程之一是有道翻译，它提供翻译服务。

    按照官方指南启用“有道翻译”。

    开发工具

    代码

    我更喜欢vscode到代码。 您可以使用 github 帐户来同步您的设置。

    我最喜欢的字体是Overpass Mono ，它应该像屏幕截图中那样：

    从命令行启动 vscode

    从终端打开 vscode 是打开新项目和更快开始编码的一种快速、简单的方法。

    将以下文本添加到您的.zshrc文件中：

     code () { VSCODE_CWD="$PWD" open -n -b "com.microsoft.VSCode" --args $* ;}

    重启终端或使用source ~/.zshrc .zshrc 后，可以通过输入code . 这应该是这样的：

    即插即用

    使用 Homebrew 安装 pnpm：

    伏打

    我使用volta来管理多个Node.js版本。

    其他软件

    异想天开

    我最喜欢的绘图应用程序。 异想天开非常棒，我完成了大部分图纸（你可以在这里和这里找到它们）。

    因为 Whimsical 已经支持PWA 。 您可以将其安装为普通应用程序。

    黑曜石

    黑曜石是我使用的笔记应用程序。

    概念

    概念是我写文章的地方。

    其他有用的软件

    • 磁铁- 窗口管理器。


原文:

This post will be continuously updated in the my github repository https://github.com/maoxiaoke/setup-a-mac-for-frontend-dev.

Setting up a new Macbook will be tough and cumbersome. Every time I am getting a new Macbook, I go over the same steps on how to set it up for my working experience.

I create a record of my setup, hoping it's helpful for others too!

Immediate Frist Steps

Enable Tap to Click

When setting up a new Macbook, one of the first changes we make is enabling the tap-to-click feature for the trackpad.

Go「System Preference -> Trackpad -> Point & Click」and enable 「Tap to click」 option.

Enable Three Finger Dragging

In general, "tap to click" on your Macbook to avoid a force-click on the trackpad. But this doesn't work when it comes to dragging and repositioning windows.

Also, I'm big fan of tree finger dragging.

  1. Go 「System Preference -> Accessibility」.
  2. In the Accessibility sidebar, choose option 「Pointer Control」.
  3. Click the button 「Trackpad Options」.
  4. Enable dragging and select 「three finger drag」.

Change Wallpaper

Visit wallhaven to find a wonderful wallpaper and replace the default ones.

Download Edge

After discovering chrome are eating my old Mac's battery, I turned to use edge.

Install Homebrew

Homebrew is "The Missing Package Manager for macOS".

Simply follow their installation guide, copy /bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)" to terminal.

For GFW reasons, the Chinese users will fail to connect to github.

Just copy /bin/zsh -c "$(curl -fsSL https://gitee.com/cunkai/HomebrewCN/raw/master/Homebrew.sh)" to your terminal. It will do the same thing when installing homebrew.

Setup Terminal

Use iTerm2 as macOS terminal replacement.

  1. Use the Homebrew to install iTerm2 
$ brew install --cask iterm2
  1. Change a few settings of iterm2
  • Go 「iterm2 Preferences -> Profiles -> Default -> Window」, add transparency and blur.

  • Go 「iterm2 Preferences -> Profiles -> Default -> Keys -> Key Mappings」, replace the standard ones with the preset "Natural Text Editing".

The great thing about this is that you can customize your shell and add plugins. Unlike the old Bash shell, which does not look attractive and can make it difficult for you to use.

$ sh -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/master/tools/install.sh)"
  1. Choose your favorite theme

There are multiple themes you can choose from. My favorite theme is honukai and it should look like in the screenshot:

For compelete guide, dive into it's docs.

  1. Add useful oh-my-zsh plugins

Oh-my-zsh is powered by plugins. I'll share my top 3 plugins.

plugins=(
 git
 autojump
 zsh-syntax-highlighting
)

The git plugin is enabled by default. It provides many aliases and a few useful functions.

To use it, add git to the plugins array in your zshrc file:

Use Homebrew to install autojump.

After that, add autojump to your zshrc file:

  1. the zsh-syntax-highlighting plugin

The installation of zsh-syntax-highlighting may be tedious. But it worth the effort.

Clone the zsh-syntax-highlighting repository in oh-my-zsh's plugins directory:

$ git clone https://github.com/zsh-users/zsh-syntax-highlighting.git ${ZSH_CUSTOM:-~/.oh-my-zsh/custom}/plugins/zsh-syntax-highlighting

Then, activate the plugin in ~/.zshrc:

plugins=(... zsh-syntax-highlighting)

After adding the plugins to your .zshrc file, you have to restart zsh to activate them, using the command source ~/.zshrc.

You can find all my settings of .zshrc here.

Install Alfred

Now, it is time for Alfred - an alternative for Spotlight.

Use Spotlight Keyboard Shortcut for Alfred

when installing Alfred, the first thing I do is disabling the default shortcut for Spotlight and use it for Alfred.

To do that, open System Preference -> Keyboard -> Shortcuts. Click on "Spotlight" and disable "Show Spotlight search".

Open the Alfred settings and use the freed up ⌘ Space combination to launch Alfred.

Clipboard History

Copy and pasting is something developers do everyday. Alfred ships with a clipboard manager which supports copied text, images and files.

The only thing I do is remapping the shortcut to ⌘ . to the Clipboard History.

Workflows

I do not have many workflows installed. One of my installed workflows is YoudaoTranslator, which provides translation service.

Follow the official guide to enable "YoudaoTranslator".

Development Tools

vscode

I prefer vscode to code. You can use github account to sync your setttings.

My favorite font is Overpass Mono and It should look like in the screenshot:

Launch vscode from the command line

Opening vscode from your terminal is a quick, easy way to open up new projects and begin coding faster.

Add the following text to your .zshrc files:

code () { VSCODE_CWD="$PWD" open -n -b "com.microsoft.VSCode" --args $* ;}

After restarting terminal or use source ~/.zshrc, you can open vscode by typing code ..

Here's how this should look:

pnpm

pnpm is fast, disk space efficient package manager, works as an alternative of npm.

Install pnpm using Homebrew:

volta

I use volta to manage multiple Node.js versions.

Other Softwares

Whimsical

My favorite drawing app out there. Whimsical is so great that I compeleted most of my drawings (You can find them here and here).

Because Whimsical support PWA already. You can install it as normal apps.

obsidian

obsidian is my note-taking app I use.

Notion

Notion is where I writing articles.

Other Useful softwares

  • Shottr - Screenshot tool, it is powerful and easy to use.
  • Anki Notes - Memory app to learn English.
  • ScreenFlow - Video editing & screen recording.
  • kap - Screen recorder to generate gif.
  • magnet - Window manager.
item.author: nazha

item.title: 我的梦想桌面设置，我没有 | My Dream Desk Setup, that I don't have
item.description:
因此，今天我将向您展示我梦想中的桌面设置。

注意，我目前没有这个桌面设置，因为我破产了。 这是我梦想中的办公桌设置，我希望有一天能拥有

他们看起来很酷，我不想在我老了的时候去看 Dr.Mike

这是最便宜的

专为编程而生

因为您无需滚动即可看到您的代码

因为如果你没有机械键盘，人们怎么会知道你在编码

罗技 MX Mini
当你只想要和平

罗技 MX Master 3
它是最受欢迎的鼠标

索尼 WH-1000XM5 耳机
你有没有听过它们，它们听起来比听其他人为你一开始导致的代码中的错误哭泣更好

质感和手感都很好

如果我能负担得起这一切，为什么不也买个耳机架

宜家台灯
我喜欢灯的形状，而且很便宜

宜家 Koppla 电源插座
因为否则我到底要如何插入所有这些

赛驰 USB-C 适配器
一些额外的端口和钱包里的一些额外的美元出了什么问题

它们只是为了展示，我永远不会使用它们。

罗技 Brio-4k 网络摄像头
我希望人们在 4k 中看到我丑陋的脸

item.author: programmingisajoke

item.title: Laravel 命令和队列作业：何时使用它们？ | Laravel commands and queue jobs: when to use them?
item.description:
您好，欢迎来到这篇博文，我想向您介绍我在 Laravel 中使用命令和队列作业的经验。

运行后台任务

命令和队列作业有助于将任务推迟到以后。 主要优点是能够不阻止最终用户执行一些长时间运行的任务。

把它想象成工单日志，当你把它们堆叠起来时，其他人可以先进入，处理它，然后继续下一个任务。

例如，让我们假设您的 Web 应用程序有一个联系页面。 它有一个用于电子邮件的文本输入，另一个用于主题，以及一个用于内容的文本区域。

提交表格时，它应该：

  1. 向您的公司邮箱发送电子邮件
  2. 向网络用户发送确认电子邮件

让我们也假设您正在使用 GMail SMTP 发送电子邮件。 在流量低的日子，这可能会很快让用户收到确认。

但是，如果每分钟发送数千个联系，这可能会使您的服务器处于高故障风险中，因为您的 CPU 使用率很高。 这也可能会恶化其他用户导航到其他页面的体验，因为服务器将忙于提供网页和发送电子邮件。

最后，您的用户可能需要等待更长的时间才能完成页面加载，然后才能看到成功确认页面。

要解决此问题，您可以要求服务器堆叠 2 个任务，一个向您的公司收件箱发送电子邮件，另一个向网络用户发送确认电子邮件。 这样，您就不会阻止 Web 用户加载成功页面，并且服务器现在可以在以后推迟任务。

命令

事实上，Laravel 需要设置一个“主”命令（称为调度程序），以便定期检查要运行的命令。

您可以控制希望命令运行的频率：

 // app/Console/Kernel.php namespace App\Console ; use Illuminate\Console\Scheduling\Schedule ; use Illuminate\Foundation\Console\Kernel as ConsoleKernel ; class Kernel extends ConsoleKernel { protected function schedule ( Schedule $schedule ): void { $schedule -> command ( 'contact-email:send' ) -> everyTenMinutes (); } }

此命令将获取尚未发送的任何联系电子邮件，并将其发送。

 // app/Console/Commands/ContactEmailSend.php namespace App\Console\Commands ; use App\Models\ContactEmail ; use App\Mail\ContactConfirmation ; use App\Mail\Contact ; use Illuminate\Console\Command ; class ContactEmailSend extends Command { protected $signature = 'contact-email:send' ; protected $description = 'Send contact email and confirmation emails.' ; public function handle () { $contactEmails = ContactEmail :: where ( "status" , "pending" ) -> get (); foreach ( $contactEmails as $contactEmail ) { $recipient = $contactEmail -> email ; $subject = $contactEmail -> subject ; $content = $contactEmail -> content ; // For the web user Mail :: to ( $recipient ) -> send ( new ContactConfirmation ( $subject , $content )); // For the company Mail :: to ( "contact@your-company.com" ) -> send ( new Contact ( $recipient , $subject , $content )); $contactEmail -> status = "sent" ; $contactEmail -> save (); } } }

有时，您可能会遇到发送电子邮件需要很长时间的情况（例如，要发送大量电子邮件时）。 目前，我们的命令应该在 10 分钟内运行，否则另一个 cron 作业将开始运行。

这不是一个想要的行为，因为如果第一个 cron 作业尚未完成发送电子邮件，则第二个 cron 作业可能会采用相同的联系电子邮件并开始尝试发送它，从而导致您的网站收到重复的确认电子邮件用户和您的公司。

要防止此行为，请增加计划频率，或指定您希望任何挂起的 cron 作业在另一个可以开始之前完成。

 // app/Console/Kernel.php namespace App\Console ; use Illuminate\Console\Scheduling\Schedule ; use Illuminate\Foundation\Console\Kernel as ConsoleKernel ; class Kernel extends ConsoleKernel { protected function schedule ( Schedule $schedule ): void { $schedule -> command ( 'contact-email:send' ) -> everyTenMinutes () -> withoutOverlapping (); // <-- } }

设置 Laravel 调度器很容易，因为每个服务器都带有一个 cronjob 运行器（可以使用crontab命令配置）。

工作

当命令更像是“每日提醒”时，我们可以将作业视为一堆要执行的任务。 当服务器有时间和资源时，服务器将一一接收。

这意味着您无法提前确定何时执行任务。

另一方面，作业需要作业运行器来解开它们（使用 Laravel 时，主管是最受欢迎的——这里有一个文档来了解如何设置它）。

但是，由于它们是一一拆开的，因此您不需要注意命令重叠。

另一个优点是您可以扩展它们，因为您可以控制有多少作业运行者取消您的作业。 这意味着如果你有一段时间知道你会有很多流量，这会导致很多人联系你的公司，你可以决定让更多的工作人员来保持低堆栈并帮助网络用户接收他们的电子邮件确认速度更快。

如果我们保持相同的示例，这里是如何运行作业的先睹为快：

 // app/Http/Controllers/ContactController.php namespace App\Http\Controllers ; use App\Jobs\SendContactEmail ; use App\Jobs\SendContactConfirmation ; use App\Http\Requests\StoreContactRequest ; class ContactController extends Controller { public function store ( StoreContactRequest $request ) { $email = $request -> email ; $subject = $request -> subject ; $content = $request -> content ; // For the company dispatch ( new SendContactEmail ( $email , $subject , $content )); // For the web user dispatch ( new SendContactConfirmation ( $email , $subject , $content )); return redirect () -> route ( "contact.success" ); } }

以下是如何向网络用户发送电子邮件：

 // app/Jobs/SendContactConfirmation.php namespace App\Jobs ; use App\Mail\ContactConfirmation ; use Illuminate\Bus\Queueable ; use Illuminate\Contracts\Queue\ShouldQueue ; use Illuminate\Foundation\Bus\Dispatchable ; use Illuminate\Queue\InteractsWithQueue ; use Illuminate\Queue\SerializesModels ; use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Mail ; class SendContactConfirmation implements ShouldQueue { use Dispatchable ; use InteractsWithQueue ; use Queueable ; use SerializesModels ; private string $email ; private string $subject ; private string $content ; public function __construct ( string $email , string $subject , string $content ) { $this -> email = $email ; $this -> subject = $subject ; $this -> content = $content ; } public function handle () { Mail :: to ( $this -> email ) -> send ( new ContactConfirmation ( $email , $subject , $content )); } }

好消息是如果发送电子邮件失败，您不必在控制器中处理它，因为它是在工作中完成的。 此外，在这种情况下，作业将被放入“failed_jobs”表中，您可以决定稍后手动重试。

如果你在一个工作中经常收到失败的电子邮件，并且你知道 SMTP 服务器经常快速关闭和启动，你可以决定让 Laravel 重试该工作：

 // app/Jobs/SendContactConfirmation.php namespace App\Jobs ; use Illuminate\Contracts\Queue\ShouldQueue ; class SendContactConfirmation implements ShouldQueue { // ... public $tries = 2 ; // retry another time before puting the job in "failed_jobs", for a total of 2 tries // ... }

您可以进行高级完整性检查，例如通过指定唯一 id确保作业在堆栈中是唯一的，或者使用WithoutOverlapping 作业中间件确保两个作业不会重叠等...

能够手动或自动重试作业的另一个优点是查看作业的状态。

使用Laravel Telescope和QUEUE_CONNECTION=database变量（在旧 Laravel 版本中称为“QUEUE_DRIVER”），您将能够准确地知道有多少任务在队列中等待，有多少失败。 这使您更容易知道您是否必须进行检查以查看问题所在，或者只是密切关注该过程。

外卖

我的个人经验告诉我，我经常会去创建一个命令，而实际上它更适合使用排队作业。 通常是由于懒惰，大多数时候是由于缺乏对关键差异的理解。

我仍然喜欢使用命令，但现在我将它们用于真正的调度任务，例如更新一次性电子邮件列表（ https://github.com/Propaganistas/Laravel-Disposable-Email ）。

最后，我们的目标是降低 HTTP 请求的负载，并在后台延迟可能延迟的内容。 这就是为什么我认为服务器的最佳设置是：

  • 一组专用于服务 HTTP 响应的服务器
  • 一组专门用于解栈作业的服务器
  • 运行计划的 cron 任务的服务器

由于计划任务服从时间，因此您不能冒险让 4 台服务器运行相同的 cron 作业（有风险）。

但是，您会看到，我们可以轻松地扩展到理论上无限数量的服务器来拆分作业。 这就是为什么大多数时候你会想找到一种方法来推迟到作业队列，因为你可以在高流量期间放置更多的服务器，然后在事情变得更安静时减少服务器的数量。

一些行业巨头已经注意到与计划任务相同的规模问题，例如 Fathom Analytics

我们曾经有积压，因为我们对汇总表使用了 INSERT ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE。 这意味着我们不能在数据进入时立即运行查询，因为你会陷入死锁战争，这会导致很多问题（相信我，有问题）。 我们遇到的问题是，我们必须将站点分组以并排运行多个 cron 作业，在隔离（按组）进程中聚合数据。 但猜猜怎么了？ 好吧，现在我们在 SingleStore 中，数据是完全实时的。 因此，如果您在网站上查看页面，它将以零延迟出现在您的 Fathom 仪表板中。

继续阅读 Fathom Analytics的构建世界上最快的网站分析博客文章。

结论

我希望我能帮助您消除关键差异，如果您已经遇到过必须在其中一种或另一种之间做出选择的情况（如果您遇到一些规模问题，那就加分！），我很想知道您的意见。

一如既往，感谢您的通读，并祝您任务安排愉快！

格伦·卡斯滕斯-彼得斯 ( Glenn Carstens-Peters ) 在Unsplash上拍摄的照片



原文:

Hi and welcome to this blog post where I would like to provide you with my experience of using commands and queue jobs within Laravel.

Running background tasks

Commands and queue jobs help deferring tasks for a later time. The main advantage is to be able to not block the end user with some long running tasks.

Think of it as ticket log, when you just stack them, and someone else is able to take the first in, process it, and move on to the next task.

For example, let us imagine your web app have a contact page. It has a text input for the email, another for the subject, and a textarea for the content.

When the form is submited, it should:

  1. Send an email to your company mailbox
  2. Send a confirmation email to the web user

Let us also imagine you are using GMail SMTP to send emails. On a low traffic day, this might be quite fast for the user to receive its confirmation.

But if there is a thousands of contact sent per minutes, this might put your server in high risk of failure because you have an high CPU usage. GMail SMTP could also rate limit you and this means chances your emails will fail to send at some point.

This can also worsen the experience of other users navigating to other pages since the server will be busy both serving web pages and sending emails.

Lastly, your users might have to wait longer before their page has finished loading before seeing the success confirmation page.

To fix this issue, you can ask your server to stack 2 tasks, one to send an email to your company inbox, another to send the confirmation email to the web user. This way, you do not block the loading of the success page for the web user, and the server will now be able to defer the task in a later time.

Commands

Commands in Laravel acts as a cron job. In fact, Laravel requires to setup a "master" command (called the Scheduler), in order to regularily check for commands to run.

You can control how often you want your command to run:

// app/Console/Kernel.php namespace App\Console; use Illuminate\Console\Scheduling\Schedule;
use Illuminate\Foundation\Console\Kernel as ConsoleKernel; class Kernel extends ConsoleKernel
{ protected function schedule(Schedule $schedule): void { $schedule ->command('contact-email:send') ->everyTenMinutes(); }
}

This commands will take any contact emails that have not been sent yet, and send them.

// app/Console/Commands/ContactEmailSend.php namespace App\Console\Commands; use App\Models\ContactEmail;
use App\Mail\ContactConfirmation;
use App\Mail\Contact;
use Illuminate\Console\Command; class ContactEmailSend extends Command
{ protected $signature = 'contact-email:send'; protected $description = 'Send contact email and confirmation emails.'; public function handle() { $contactEmails = ContactEmail::where("status", "pending")->get(); foreach ($contactEmails as $contactEmail) { $recipient = $contactEmail->email; $subject = $contactEmail->subject; $content = $contactEmail->content; // For the web user Mail::to($recipient)->send(new ContactConfirmation($subject, $content)); // For the company Mail::to("contact@your-company.com")->send(new Contact($recipient, $subject, $content)); $contactEmail->status = "sent"; $contactEmail->save(); } }
}

Sometimes you can end up in situations when sending emails takes a long time (for example when there is a ton of emails to send). For the moment, our commands should run within 10 minutes or another cron job will start running.

It is not a wanted behavior because if the first cron job has not finished to send an email, the second cron job might take the same contact email and start to try to send it as well, leading to duplicate confirmation emails being received by your web users and your company.

To prevent this behavior, either increase the schedule frequency, or specify you want any pending cron job to finish before another can start.

// app/Console/Kernel.php namespace App\Console; use Illuminate\Console\Scheduling\Schedule;
use Illuminate\Foundation\Console\Kernel as ConsoleKernel; class Kernel extends ConsoleKernel
{ protected function schedule(Schedule $schedule): void { $schedule ->command('contact-email:send') ->everyTenMinutes() ->withoutOverlapping(); // <-- }
}

Setting up the Laravel Scheduler is easy since every servers comes with a cronjob runner (configurable using the crontab command).

Jobs

When commands are more like "daily reminders", we can see jobs as a stack of tasks to do. The server will take them one by one, when it has some time and resources.

This means you cannot determine in advance when the task will be performed.

In the other hand jobs requires a job runner to unstack them (Supervisor being the most popular when using Laravel - a documentation is available here to know how to set it up).

However, since they are unstacked one by one, you do not need to take care of command overlapping.

The other advantage is that you can scale them, because you can control how many job runners unstack your jobs. This means if you have a period of time you know you will have a lot of traffic, which will lead to lots of people to contact your company, you can decide to put more job runners to keep the stack low and help web users to receive their email confirmation faster.

If we keep the same example, here is a sneak peak of how to run a job:

// app/Http/Controllers/ContactController.php namespace App\Http\Controllers; use App\Jobs\SendContactEmail;
use App\Jobs\SendContactConfirmation;
use App\Http\Requests\StoreContactRequest; class ContactController extends Controller
{ public function store(StoreContactRequest $request) { $email = $request->email; $subject = $request->subject; $content = $request->content; // For the company dispatch(new SendContactEmail($email, $subject, $content)); // For the web user dispatch(new SendContactConfirmation($email, $subject, $content)); return redirect()->route("contact.success"); }
}

And here is how to send an email to the web user:

// app/Jobs/SendContactConfirmation.php namespace App\Jobs; use App\Mail\ContactConfirmation;
use Illuminate\Bus\Queueable;
use Illuminate\Contracts\Queue\ShouldQueue;
use Illuminate\Foundation\Bus\Dispatchable;
use Illuminate\Queue\InteractsWithQueue;
use Illuminate\Queue\SerializesModels;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Mail; class SendContactConfirmation implements ShouldQueue
{ use Dispatchable; use InteractsWithQueue; use Queueable; use SerializesModels; private string $email; private string $subject; private string $content; public function __construct(string $email, string $subject, string $content) { $this->email = $email; $this->subject = $subject; $this->content = $content; } public function handle() { Mail::to($this->email)->send(new ContactConfirmation($email, $subject, $content)); }
}

The good thing is if sending an email failed, you do not have to handle it in the controller since it is done on the job. Also, in this case, the job will be put in the "failed_jobs" table, and you can decide to retry it later manually.

If you often have failed emails within a job, and you know the SMTP server often goes down and up quick, you could decide to ask Laravel to retry the job:

// app/Jobs/SendContactConfirmation.php namespace App\Jobs; use Illuminate\Contracts\Queue\ShouldQueue; class SendContactConfirmation implements ShouldQueue
{ // ... public $tries = 2; // retry another time before puting the job in "failed_jobs", for a total of 2 tries // ...
}

You can do advanced integrity checks like making sure the job is unique in the stack by specifying a unique id, or making sure two jobs will not overlap using the WithoutOverlapping job middleware, etc...

The other advantage with being able to retry your job manually or automatically is to see the state of your jobs.

Using Laravel Telescope coupled with the QUEUE_CONNECTION=database env variable (called "QUEUE_DRIVER" in older Laravel version), you will be able to know exactly how many tasks are waiting in the queue, how many failed. This makes it easiers to know if you have to give a check to see what's wrong or just keep an eye open on the process.

Takeway

My personal experience have taught me I would often go to creating a command when in fact it would have been more suited to use a queued job. Often by lazyness, most of the time by lack of understanding the key differences.

I still like to use commands, but now I dedicate them for real scheduling tasks like updating a list of disposable emails (https://github.com/Propaganistas/Laravel-Disposable-Email).

In the end our goal is to lower the load for the HTTP requests, and deffer what's possible to deffer in the background. That is why I think the optimal setup for a server would be:

  • a fleet of servers dedicated to serve HTTP responses
  • a fleet of servers dedicated to unstack jobs
  • a server to run scheduled cron tasks

Since scheduled tasks obeys to a time, you cannot take the risk of having 4 servers running the same cron job (risky).

However, you see we can easily scale up to a theorically infinite number of servers to unstack jobs. That is why most of the time you will want to find a way to deffer to the job queue because you are able to put more servers during a high traffic period, and then lower the number of servers when things get more quiet.

Some giants of the industry have noticed the same issues of scale with scheduled tasks, like Fathom Analytics

We used to have a backlog, as we used INSERT ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE for our summary tables. This meant we couldn't just run queries as soon as data came in, as you get into a DEADLOCK war, and it causes a lot of problems (believe me, had them). And the problem we had was that we had to put sites into groups to run multiple cron jobs side by side, aggregating the data in isolated (by group) processes. But guess what? Cron jobs don't scale, and we were starting to see bigger pageview backlogs each day. Well, now we're in SingleStore, data is fully real-time. So if you view a page on your website, it will appear in your Fathom dashboard with zero delays.

Keep reading on Building the world's fastest website analytics blog post of Fathom Analytics.

Conclusion

I hope I helped you demistifying the key differences, and I am very curious to know your opinion if you have already crossed situations when you had to make a choice between one or another (bonus point if you had some scale issues with it!).

As always, thanks for reading through, and happy task scheduling!

Photo by Glenn Carstens-Peters on Unsplash
item.author: khalyomede

item.title: Laravel 队列与主管 - Docker 配置 | Laravel queues with supervisor - Docker Configuration
item.description:
我正在为那些在使用 docker 与主管设置队列时遇到困难的人写这篇博客。

因此，我们将创建一个单独的 docker 服务，以供主管执行更好的性能。

首先我们需要配置主docker-compose.yml ：

 supervisor: build: context: . dockerfile: {path to file}/supervisor.dockerfile container_name: supervisor volumes: - ./src:/var/www/html networks: - laravel

然后需要为主管supervisor.dockerfile创建一个单独的 dockerfile

 FROM php:7.4-fpm-alpine // I'm using 7.4 you can use php-version as per your application. RUN docker-php-ext-install pdo pdo_mysql RUN apk update && apk add --no-cache supervisor RUN mkdir -p "/etc/supervisor/logs" COPY {path to file}/supervisord.conf /etc/supervisor/supervisord.conf CMD ["/usr/bin/supervisord", "-n", "-c", "/etc/supervisor/supervisord.conf"]

之后，我们需要创建一个supervisord.conf配置文件并在其中设置日志目录等。

 [supervisord] logfile=/etc/supervisor/logs/supervisord.log ; main log file; default $CWD/supervisord.log logfile_maxbytes=5MB ; max main logfile bytes b4 rotation; default 50MB logfile_backups=10 ; # of main logfile backups; 0 means none, default 10 loglevel=info ; log level; default info; others: debug,warn,trace pidfile=/tmp/supervisord.pid ; supervisord pidfile; default supervisord.pid nodaemon=false ; start in foreground if true; default false minfds=1024 ; min. avail startup file descriptors; default 1024 minprocs=200 ; min. avail process descriptors;default 200 [rpcinterface:supervisor] supervisor.rpcinterface_factory = supervisor.rpcinterface:make_main_rpcinterface [supervisorctl] serverurl=unix:///tmp/supervisor.sock ; use a unix:// URL for a unix socket [program:laravel-worker] process_name=%(program_name)s_%(process_num)02d command=php /var/www/html/artisan queue:work autostart=true autorestart=true stopasgroup=true killasgroup=true numprocs=1 redirect_stderr=true stdout_logfile=/var/www/html/storage/logs/worker.log stopwaitsecs=3600 stdout_logfile_maxbytes=5MB

就是这样。

现在运行并重建 docker 容器，它就像繁荣一样工作💥

谢谢阅读。

** If you have any question so you can ask in comment section ** 有什么问题可以在评论区提问



原文:

I am writing this blog for those people who are facing difficulties for setting up the queues with supervisor using docker.

So we'll going to create a separate service of docker for supervisor to do better performance.

first we need to configure the main docker-compose.yml :

supervisor:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: {path to file}/supervisor.dockerfile
    container_name: supervisor
    volumes:
      - ./src:/var/www/html
    networks:
      - laravel

then need to create a separate dockerfile for supervisor supervisor.dockerfile

FROM php:7.4-fpm-alpine // I'm using 7.4 you can use php-version as per your application.

RUN docker-php-ext-install pdo pdo_mysql

RUN apk update && apk add --no-cache supervisor

RUN mkdir -p "/etc/supervisor/logs"

COPY {path to file}/supervisord.conf /etc/supervisor/supervisord.conf

CMD ["/usr/bin/supervisord", "-n", "-c",  "/etc/supervisor/supervisord.conf"]

after that we need to create a supervisord.conf configuration file and set the logs directory and etc... in it.

[supervisord]
logfile=/etc/supervisor/logs/supervisord.log ; main log file; default $CWD/supervisord.log
logfile_maxbytes=5MB         ; max main logfile bytes b4 rotation; default 50MB
logfile_backups=10           ; # of main logfile backups; 0 means none, default 10
loglevel=info                ; log level; default info; others: debug,warn,trace
pidfile=/tmp/supervisord.pid ; supervisord pidfile; default supervisord.pid
nodaemon=false               ; start in foreground if true; default false
minfds=1024                  ; min. avail startup file descriptors; default 1024
minprocs=200                 ; min. avail process descriptors;default 200

[rpcinterface:supervisor]
supervisor.rpcinterface_factory = supervisor.rpcinterface:make_main_rpcinterface

[supervisorctl]
serverurl=unix:///tmp/supervisor.sock ; use a unix:// URL  for a unix socket

[program:laravel-worker]
process_name=%(program_name)s_%(process_num)02d
command=php /var/www/html/artisan queue:work
autostart=true
autorestart=true
stopasgroup=true
killasgroup=true
numprocs=1
redirect_stderr=true
stdout_logfile=/var/www/html/storage/logs/worker.log
stopwaitsecs=3600
stdout_logfile_maxbytes=5MB

and that's it.

now run & rebuild the docker container and it's worked like a boom 💥

Thanks for reading.

** If you have any question so you can ask in comment section

item.title: Svelte 应用程序：使用 svelte/motion 创建 Loading Overlay 组件 | Svelte app: create Loading Overlay component using svelte/motion
item.description:
对于我的一个项目，我需要创建一个加载覆盖组件来隐藏应用程序，直到加载完成。 在这篇文章中，我想分享我是如何做到的。

首先，让我们勾勒出 HTML 和 CSS 标记。 这很简单。 我创建了一个块，将其固定放置，将其拉伸到全屏大小，并将其内容居中。 注意我们如何使用 svg 渐变来填充不同颜色的文本。

 <div class= "overlay" > <svg width= "115" height= "32" viewBox= "0 0 78 32" xmlns= "http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" > <defs> <linearGradient id= "filler" > <stop offset= "50%" stop-color= "#ff006e" /> <stop offset= "50%" stop-color= "white" /> </linearGradient> </defs> <text x= "0" y= "24px" fill= "url(#filler)" > Loading... </text> </svg> </div> <style> .overlay { position : fixed ; top : 0 ; right : 0 ; bottom : 0 ; left : 0 ; z-index : 10001 ; display : flex ; align-items : center ; justify-content : center ; background : #3a86ff ; font-family : "Arial" , sans-serif ; font-size : 1.375rem ; } </style>

凉爽的。 我们完成了标记。 现在让我们继续动画。 为此，我们将使用标准 Svelte 工具。 使用svelte/motion的tweened ，我们创建了一个progress变量，并在 svg 渐变中使用它的值。

 const progress = tweened ( 0 , { easing : linear , duration : 3000 });
 <linearGradient id= "filler" > <stop offset= {`${$progress}%`} stop-color= "#ff006e" /> <stop offset= {`${$progress}%`} stop-color= "white" /> </linearGradient>

接下来，让我们创建我们将在组件挂载时调用的animate函数。 我们使这个函数递归，以便动画无限期地继续。 像任何其他递归函数一样，它必须有一个退出点。 因此，为了避免内存泄漏，我们确保函数的执行在组件实例被销毁时结束。

 let destroyed = false async function animate () { if ( destroyed ) { return } await width . set ( 100 ) await width . set ( 0 , { duration : 0 }) await animate () } onMount (() => { animate () }) onDestroy (() => { destroyed = true })

美好的。 我们的组件看起来已经不错了。 现在我们将通过两阶段改进生成的动画，在第一阶段我们用粉红色在白色文本上绘制，反之亦然，在第 2 阶段我们用白色在粉红色文本上绘制。 为此，我们将创建一个带有布尔值的writable存储，我们将使用它来指示动画的当前阶段。 每次调用animate函数时，我们都会将此值更改为相反的值。

 const isFirstStage = writable ( true ) async function animate () { if ( destroyed ) { return } await width . set ( 100 ) await width . set ( 0 , { duration : 0 }) isFirstStage . update (( value ) => ! value ) await animate () }
 <linearGradient id= "filler" > <stop offset= {`${$progress}%`} stop-color= {$isFirstStage ? ' #ff006e ' : ' white '} /> <stop offset= {`${$progress}%`} stop-color= {$isFirstStage ? ' white ' : ' #ff006e '} /> </linearGradient>

因此，我们完成了加载指示器组件本身。 现在让我们假设可以有几个组件使用我们的指标。 我们可以在它们中的每一个中渲染我们的叠加层，但让我们更好地改进我们的方法并创建允许我们渲染一次指示器并提供打开和关闭指示器的函数的功能。 为此，我们将创建另一个writable存储，其中将包含显示指标的请求。 只要商店中至少还有一个请求，就会呈现指示器本身。

 import { writable , derived } from " svelte/store " ; import { nanoid } from " nanoid " ; export const loadingOverlayQueue = writable ([]); export function showLoadingOverlay () { const newRequestId = nanoid (); loadingOverlayQueue . update (( currentValue ) => [... currentValue , newRequestId ]); return newRequestId ; } export function hideLoadingOverlay ( requestId ) { loadingOverlayQueue . update (( currentValue ) => currentValue . filter (( item ) => item !== requestId ) ); } export const isLoadingOverlayShown = derived ( loadingOverlayQueue , ( requests ) => requests . length > 0 );

现在，一切都完成了。 我们通过几个简单的步骤创建了一个原始的动画加载指示器。 源代码可以在我的 GitHub 上找到。 你喜欢和 svelte 一起工作吗？



原文:

For one of my projects, I needed to create a loading overlay component to hide the app until the loading is complete. In this article, I want to share how I did it.

First, let's sketch out the HTML and CSS markup. This is quite simple. I create a block, position it fixed, stretch it to full-screen size, and centre its content. Notice how we use the svg gradient to fill the text with different colours.

<div class="overlay"> <svg width="115" height="32" viewBox="0 0 78 32" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"> <defs> <linearGradient id="filler"> <stop offset="50%" stop-color="#ff006e" /> <stop offset="50%" stop-color="white" /> </linearGradient> </defs> <text x="0" y="24px" fill="url(#filler)">Loading...</text> </svg>
</div> <style> .overlay { position: fixed; top: 0; right: 0; bottom: 0; left: 0; z-index: 10001; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; background: #3a86ff; font-family: "Arial", sans-serif; font-size: 1.375rem; }
</style>

Cool. We are done with the markup. Now let's move on to animation. To do this, we will use standard Svelte tools. Using tweened from svelte/motion we create a progress variable and use its value in the svg gradient.

const progress = tweened(0, { easing: linear, duration: 3000 });

 <linearGradient id="filler"> <stop offset={`${$progress}%`} stop-color="#ff006e" /> <stop offset={`${$progress}%`} stop-color="white" /> </linearGradient>

Next, let's create animate function that we will call when the component mounts. We make this function recursive so that the animation continues indefinitely. Like any other recursive function, it must have an exit point. Therefore, in order to avoid memory leaks, we make sure that the execution of the function ends when the component instance is destroyed.

 let destroyed = false async function animate() { if (destroyed) { return } await width.set(100) await width.set(0, { duration: 0 }) await animate() } onMount(() => { animate() }) onDestroy(() => { destroyed = true })

Fine. Our component already looks good. Now we will improve the resulting animation by making it two-stage, where in the first stage we paint over the white text with pink, and vice versa, in stage number 2 we paint over the pink text with white. To do this, we will create a writable store with a boolean value that we will use to indicate the current stage of the animation. With each call of the animate function, we will change this value to the opposite.

 const isFirstStage = writable(true) async function animate() { if (destroyed) { return } await width.set(100) await width.set(0, { duration: 0 }) isFirstStage.update((value) => !value) await animate() }

<linearGradient id="filler"> <stop offset={`${$progress}%`} stop-color={$isFirstStage ? '#ff006e' : 'white'} /> <stop offset={`${$progress}%`} stop-color={$isFirstStage ? 'white' : '#ff006e'} />
</linearGradient>

So, we are done with the loading indicator component itself. Now let's imagine that there can be several components that use our indicator. We could render our overlay in each of them, but let's better refine our approach and create functionality that will allow us to render the indicator once and provide functions for turning the indicator on and off. To do this, we will create another writable store, which will contain requests to display the indicator. The indicator itself will be rendered as long as there is at least one request left in the store.

import { writable, derived } from "svelte/store";
import { nanoid } from "nanoid"; export const loadingOverlayQueue = writable([]); export function showLoadingOverlay() { const newRequestId = nanoid(); loadingOverlayQueue.update((currentValue) => [...currentValue, newRequestId]); return newRequestId;
} export function hideLoadingOverlay(requestId) { loadingOverlayQueue.update((currentValue) => currentValue.filter((item) => item !== requestId) );
} export const isLoadingOverlayShown = derived( loadingOverlayQueue, (requests) => requests.length > 0
);

Now, all done. We've created an original animated loading indicator in a few easy steps. The source code can be found on my GitHub. Do you enjoy working with svelte?
item.author: pavlov

item.title: Android Pentesting：关于 Parrot OS 的 DIVA 不安全日志记录和硬编码问题的记录 | Android Pentesting: Writeup of the DIVA Insecure Logging and Hardcoding Issues for Parrot OS
item.description:
如果你读过我以前的教程（或文章），你就会知道我已经开始涉足黑客世界，我最近的文章主要集中在 Android Pentesting。 我已经向您展示了如何在 Parrot OS 上安装Genymotion 和 Virtualbox 、 JDX-GUI 和 ADB以及Android Studio - 但今天我们将不仅仅只是安装工具。 今天我们开始 hacking，有点.

首先，我需要确保我们在同一页面上。 什么是安卓渗透测试？ 简而言之，它是针对移动应用程序的模拟网络攻击，我们试图暴露并发现应用程序中存在的任何漏洞或安全问题。 在接下来的几天里，我们将对DIVA APK进行渗透测试，我将在本教程中解释并演示如何在模拟设备上安装和设置。

在我们开始之前，请确保您具备以下条件：

  • 已安装 Android Studio。
  • 安装了 Genymotion 和 Virtualbox，具有模拟设备设置和运行。
  • 好吧，让我们开始黑客攻击！ 当我们在我们的设备上打开 DIVA 应用程序时，我们可以看到它由一个菜单或导航组成，其中列出了许多供我们进行 Pentest 的部分。

    在本节中，我们将测试此应用程序的不安全日志记录和硬编码问题（第 1 部分和第 2 部分）部分。

    不安全的日志记录

    当我们打开不安全日志记录部分时，我们会遇到以下目标：找出在何处/如何记录的内容以及易受攻击的代码。 因此，我们应该找到正在记录的内容、记录方式以及易受攻击的代码。
    让我们尝试一一回答这些目标。 从第一印象来看，只需查看活动布局（即我们屏幕上打开的页面），我们就可以看到它要求我们提供信用卡号码。 因此，我们的信用卡号可能就是正在记录的数据（什么）。 让我们通过输入一个数字来测试它！


    输入我们的号码后，我们会收到一个错误弹出窗口。 让我们看看我们是否可以看到它是如何被记录的，即。 我们的数据去哪儿了？ 我们可以看到的第一个罪魁祸首是Android Studio 中的 LogCat 。 打开 Android Studio 并导航到 LogCat 输出。 我们可以看到它记录了所有内容，这很糟糕，因为假设我们有一个用户下载了一个恶意 APK，该 APK 在他们使用该应用程序时秘密监控他们的日志 - 然后攻击者可以使用这个监控的日志来读取敏感数据，并在这边走。

当我们向下滚动时，我们看到我们的信用卡号已被记录。 糟糕的LOGCAT！ 现在我们知道它是如何被记录的，通过 Android Studio 中标记为 diva-log 的日志下的 LogCat 输出。

最后，我们需要找到易受攻击的代码。 简单，只需将您的DIVA APK 加载到 JDX-GUI并打开LogActivity活动。
当我们查看代码时，我们可以识别它进行实际记录的部分，从而确认它正在记录我们的信用卡号。 也许记录敏感信息不是捕获此信息的最佳方式？

因此，总而言之，我们能够找到以下内容：

  • 什么：信用卡号
  • 其中：LogCat > diva-log
  • 如何：Log.e()

硬编码问题第一部分

当程序员硬编码一个值时，这意味着他们将其键入到应用程序中，使其成为静态值。 例如：

 var password = "iamnotsecure";

这不仅从编程的角度来看是不好的，因为开发人员每次想要更新它时都必须手动更改这个值，而且从安全的角度来看，它允许攻击者轻松利用或劫持固件、设备、系统和软件。

当我们打开硬编码问题第 1 部分时，我们会遇到以下目标：找出硬编码的内容和位置。 因此，我们希望找到硬编码的内容和位置。 从一开始，我们就可以立即确定将被硬编码的内容：供应商密钥。

到哪里去。 让我们进入 JDX-GUI 并打开HardcodeActivity活动，看看我们是否可以在源代码中找到任何东西。

立即我们可以看到供应商密钥是硬编码的，因此我们可以复制“ vendorsecretkey ”值并将其弹出到我们的应用程序中。

瞧，我们已经获得了对系统的访问权限！ 因此，总而言之，我们能够找到以下内容：

  • 内容：供应商密钥 > vendorsecretkey
  • 哪里：硬编码活动

硬编码问题第二部分

当我们打开硬编码问题第二部分部分时，我们会遇到以下目标：找出硬编码的内容和位置。 因此，我们希望找到硬编码的内容和位置。 就像以前一样，我们可以通过查看活动布局立即识别可能是硬编码的内容：供应商密钥。

现在到哪里。 让我们进入 JDX-GUI 中的Hardcode2Activity并打开它。 我们可以立即识别出被引用的DivaJni类，因为其余代码依赖于该类来验证访问密钥。

不浪费更多时间，让我们打开 DivaJni 类。 当我第一次打开它时，没有正确评估代码，我看到了字符串 soName = "divajni"，最初我认为这是硬编码的值。 令我沮丧的是，事实并非如此。 傻我，也许下次我应该完整阅读源代码！ 通过进一步检查，我们可以看到静态函数加载了soName的本机库，这是一个名为divajni的库。 由于我们可以假设它调用Shared Library ，我们可以去寻找一个名为“divajni.so”的文件。 嗯，所以我们在 APK 文件的 lib 文件夹中寻找包含“divajni.so”的库。 转到您安装 DIVA APK 的位置并提取文件（要轻松完成此操作，只需将 .apk 替换为 .zip 并像平常一样提取）。 解压后，打开它并转到lib > 任何一个文件夹 > libdivajni.so 。

您可以将此库文件复制到您的桌面或任何易于访问的地方。 使用 CTRL + ALT + T 打开终端并cd进入 Destkop。 通过输入ls命令确保您的 libdivajni.so 文件存在。

为了能够查看二进制文件或数据文件（例如我们的库文件）中的文本，我们需要使用strings命令。 输入它： strings libdivajni.so 。 我们可以看到弹出的单词列表，主要是包和扩展库。 如果你看一下，它们中的大多数都有类似的格式：.x、_x、__x、x.so 等。有一个字符串让我印象深刻，因为它为什么会有一个分号？ 为什么？ 其他字符串都没有分号！

让我们尝试输入它作为我们的供应商密钥。 请注意，此过程是反复试验。 有时我们可能不会像在这种情况下那样幸运地在字符串列表中识别出明显的异常值。 如果我们将它输入到我们的应用程序中，我们会弹出一个窗口，说我们已获得访问权限，并且成功！

因此，总而言之，我们能够找到以下内容：

  • 什么：供应商密钥
  • 其中：libdivajni.so > olsdfgad;lh

结论

恭喜，我们已经完成了 DIVA APK 的前两个部分！ 我希望这很容易理解/理解。 下次见第 3 节：不安全的数据存储。😊

如果您对我也可以遵循的任何很酷的工具、技术或教程有建议，请随时将它们留在下面，我会检查出来！

技术解决方案 - Jan 20 1月20日

whatminjacodes [she/they] - whatminjacodes [她/他们] - Jan 18 1月18日

Mahmoud Mabrok Fouad - Mahmoud Mabrok Fouad - Jan 17 1月17日



原文:

If you've read my previous tutorials(or write-ups), you would know that I have started dabbling in the world of hacking, with my most recent write-ups focusing primarily on Android Pentesting. I've shown you how to install Genymotion & Virtualbox, JDX-GUI & ADB, and Android Studio on Parrot OS - but today we will go beyond just installing tools. Today we start hacking, kind of.

First things first, I need to make sure that we are on the same page. What is Android Pentesting? Simply put, it is a simulated cyber attack against a mobile application where we try to expose and find any vulnerabilities or security issues that are present in the application. In the following days to come, we will be Pentesting the DIVA APK, which I explain and demonstrate how to install and set up on an emulated device in this tutorial.

Before we start make sure that you have the following:

  • Android Studio Installed.
  • Genymotion and Virtualbox installed, with an emulated device setup and running.
  • ADB is installed with DIVA APK installed onto the device.
  • JDX-GUI is installed and open.

Okay, let's get hacking! When we open the DIVA application on our device, we can see that it consists of a menu or navigation that lists many sections for us to Pentest.

In this section, we will be testing the Insecure Logging and Hardcoding Issues (Part 1 & 2) sections of this application.

Insecure Logging

When we open the Insecure Logging section we are met with the following objective: find out what is being logged where/how and the vulnerable code. So, we are expected to find what is being logged, how it is logged, and the vulnerable code.
Let's try and answer these objectives one by one. From first impressions, just by looking at the activity layout (which is the page opened on our screen), we can see that it asks us for our credit card numbers. Thus, our credit card number is probably the data that is being logged (what). Let's test this by entering a number!


With our number entered, we get an error popup. Let's see if we can see how it is being logged, ie. where did our data go? The first culprit that we can look at is LogCat in Android Studio. Open up Android Studio and navigate over to the LogCat Output. We can see that it logs everything, which is bad because say we have a user that downloads a malicious APK that secretly monitors their log when they use the app - the attacker can then use this monitored log to read sensitive data, and exploit them in this way.

When we scroll down, we see that our credit card number has been logged. BAD LOGCAT! Now we know how it is being logged, via the LogCat Output in Android Studio underneath a log labeled diva-log.

Finally, we need to find the vulnerable code. Easy peasy, just load up your DIVA APK into JDX-GUI and open up the LogActivity activity.
When we look at the code, we can identify the section where it does the actual logging, thus confirming that it is logging our credit card number. Maybe logging sensitive information is not the best way to capture this information?

So, in summary, we were able to find the following:

  • What: Credit Card Number
  • Where: LogCat > diva-log
  • How: Log.e()

Hardcoding Issues Part One

When a programmer hardcodes a value, it means that they type it into the application, making it a static value. For example:

var password = "iamnotsecure";

This is not only bad from a programming perspective since the developer will have to manually change this value every time they want to update it, but from a security perspective, it allows attackers to easily exploit or hijack firmware, devices, systems, and software.

When we open the Hardcoding Issues Part One section we are met with the following objective: find out what is hardcoded and where. So, we are expected to find what is being hardcoded and where. From the get-go, we can immediately identify what will be hardcoded: the vendor key.

On to the where. Let's head into JDX-GUI and open up the HardcodeActivity activity to see if we can find anything in the source code.

Immediately we can see that the vendor key is hardcoded, thus we can copy the "vendorsecretkey" value and pop it into our app.

Voila, we have gained access to the system! 🥳

So, in summary, we were able to find the following:

  • What: Vendor Secret Key > vendorsecretkey
  • Where: HardcodeActivity

Hardcoding Issues Part Two

When we open the Hardcoding Issues Part Two section we are met with the following objective: find out what is hardcoded and where. So, we are expected to find what is being hardcoded and where. Just like previously, we can immediately identify what is probably hardcoded just by looking at the activity layout: the vendor key.

Now on to the where. Let's head into our Hardcode2Activity in JDX-GUI and open it up. We can immediately identify the DivaJni class that is being referenced, as the rest of the code depends on this class to be able to validate the access key.

Without wasting further time, let's open the DivaJni class. When I first opened it, without properly assessing the code, I saw the string soName = "divajni", and initially I thought this was the hardcoded value. To my dismay, it was not. Silly me, maybe next time I should read the source code fully! 😂

We can see upon further inspection that the static function loads the native library of soName, which is a library named divajni. Since we can assume that it calls upon a Shared Library, we can go looking for a file named "divajni.so". The .so extension identifies a Shared Object library that may be dynamically loaded during Android runtime.

Mmh, so we are looking for a library containing "divajni.so" in our lib folder within our APK file. Go to where you installed your DIVA APK and extract the file (to do this easily, just replace .apk with .zip and extract like normal). Once extracted, open it up and go lib > any one of the folders > libdivajni.so.

You can copy this library file to your Desktop or anywhere with easy access. Open up your terminal using CTRL + ALT + T and cd into Destkop. Make sure your libdivajni.so file is there by entering the ls command.

To be able to view the text inside a binary or data file such as our library file, we need to use the strings command. Enter it as such: strings libdivajni.so. We can see a list of words popping up, mainly packages and extended libraries. If you look at it, most of them have a similar format: .x, _x, __x, x.so, etc. There is one string that stood out to me, because why would it have a semi-colon? Why? None of the other strings have semi-colons!

Let's try entering it as our vendor key. Please note that this process is trial-and-error. Sometimes we might not be so lucky as to identify a clear outlier in a string list as in this scenario. If we enter it into our app, we get a popup saying we have gained access, and success!

So, in summary, we were able to find the following:

  • What: Vendor Secret Key
  • Where: libdivajni.so > olsdfgad;lh

Conclusion

Congrats, we have finished the first two sections of the DIVA APK! I hope this was easy enough to follow/understand. I'll see you next time with Section 3: Insecure Data Storage.😊

If you have recommendations on any cool tools, techniques, or tutorials that I too can follow feel free to leave them below and I'll check it out!

(Pull this on my GitHub for future reference)

Techtic Solutions - Jan 20

whatminjacodes [she/they] - Jan 18

Mahmoud Mabrok Fouad - Jan 17

item.title: Máy Hút Ẩm Không Khí Kosmen KM-150S Cao Cấp | Máy Hút Ẩm Không Khí Kosmen KM-150S Cao Cấp
item.description:
歌曲歌曲，cácTínhNănngtíchhợpthôngminhnhưtựtự

清宣南，Q。 清宣，Tp。 河内。 副总裁Đà Nẵng：Số 385B Hải Phòng、P. Tân Chính、Q. Thanh Khê、TP。 热线：𝟎𝟕𝟔𝟔 𝟖𝟗𝟗𝟕𝟗𝟗 (Miền Bắc) - 𝟎𝟖𝟖𝟖 𝟕𝟖𝟕 𝟗𝟓𝟗 (Miền Nam)

网站：http: //kosmen.vn


粉丝专页： https ://www.facebook.com/kosmenvn/
文格尔： https ://www.vingle.net/kosmenvietnam


原文:

Với công dụng và tính năng vượt trội, máy hút ẩm công suất lớn Kosmen KM-150S luôn phát huy và đáp ứng mục đích sử dụng trong đa dạng lĩnh vực. Thiết bị mang tính ứng dụng cao với giá thành cạnh tranh, kiểm soát ẩm tốt, đồng thời tích hợp chức năng lọc không khí và sấy vải, sản phẩm là sự lựa chọn được nhiều quý khách hàng tin dùng trong nhà máy, kho bãi quy mô vừa và lớn, không gian 120 - 200m².

Với trang bị bảng điều khiển kỹ thuật số kèm màn hình LED hiện đại và tiên tiến, bạn có thể dễ dàng sử dụng và tùy chỉnh chế độ phù hợp. Song song đó, các tính năng tích hợp thông minh như tự động rã đông, bảo vệ quá tải cũng giúp bạn ngăn chặn, kiểm soát và xử lý tốt những tình huống xấu có thể xảy đến.

Ngoài ra, một ưu điểm không thể không kể đến của thiết bị này chính là độ ồn có tần số khá thấp (≤ 65dB), không gây nhiễu và ảnh hưởng đến mọi người trong quá trình hoạt động, có thể ứng dụng cả trong không gian sảnh, thư viện, trường học hay phòng y tế,...

Xem thêm chi tiết sản phẩm: https://kosmen.vn/may-hut-am-cong-nghiep-kosmen-km-150s KOSMEN - MÁY HÚT ẨM THƯƠNG HIỆU ĐỨC Trụ sở chính: 27C đường số 12, P. Hiệp Bình Phước, TP Thủ Đức, TP.HCM VP Hà Nội: Số 3 Ngõ 495 Nguyễn Trãi, p. Thanh Xuân Nam, q. Thanh Xuân, Tp. Hà Nội. VP Đà Nẵng: Số 385B Hải Phòng, P. Tân Chính, Q. Thanh Khê, TP. Đà Nẵng. Hotline: 𝟎𝟕𝟔𝟔 𝟖𝟗𝟗𝟕𝟗𝟗 (Miền Bắc) - 𝟎𝟖𝟖𝟖 𝟕𝟖𝟕 𝟗𝟓𝟗 (Miền Nam)
